{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_78a1dd99626b4f2e94ce5035","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! CDC (Change Data Capture) — plan §13.13.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Publishes document change events to configured sinks (webhook, NATS, Kafka, internal queue).\n4\t//!\n5\t//! # Origin Tag-Based Event Suppression\n6\t//!\n7\t//! The `_miroir_origin` tag is an **internal orchestrator-side marker** used to suppress\n8\t//! internal write events from CDC by default. It is:\n9\t//! - Never stored on documents\n10\t//! - Never returned to clients\n11\t//! - Never transmitted outside the orchestrator process\n12\t//!\n13\t//! ## Suppression Rules (plan §13.13)\n14\t//!\n15\t//! | Origin Tag | Write Type | Default Behavior | Opt-In Via |\n16\t//! |------------|------------|-------------------|------------|\n17\t//! | `None` | Client write | **Always emitted** | N/A |\n18\t//! | `ANTIENTROPY` | Anti-entropy repair | Suppressed | `emit_internal_writes` |\n19\t//! | `RESHARD_BACKFILL` | Reshard backfill | Suppressed | `emit_internal_writes` |\n20\t//! | `ROLLOVER` | ILM rollover | Suppressed | `emit_internal_writes` |\n21\t//! | `TTL_EXPIRE` | TTL expiration delete | Suppressed | `emit_ttl_deletes` |\n22\t//!\n23\t//! ## Usage\n24\t//!\n25\t//! Set the origin tag when constructing `WriteRequest`:\n26\t//! ```ignore\n27\t//! // Client write (always emitted to CDC)\n28\t//! WriteRequest { origin: None, .. }\n29\t//!\n30\t//! // Anti-entropy repair write (suppressed by default)\n31\t//! WriteRequest { origin: Some(ORIGIN_ANTIENTROPY.to_string()), .. }\n32\t//! ```\n33\t\n34\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n35\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n36\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n37\tuse `emit_internal_writes` is true.\n42\tpub const ORIGIN_ANTIENTROPY: &str = \"antientropy\";\n43\t\n44\t/// Origin tag for reshard backfill writes (plan §13.1 steps 2-3).\n45\t/// These writes are suppressed from CDC unless `emit_internal_writes` is true.\n46\tpub const ORIGIN_RESHARD_BACKFILL: &str = \"reshard_backfill\";\n47\t\n48\t/// Origin tag for ILM rollover writes (plan §13.17).\n49\t/// These writes are suppressed from CDC unless `emit_internal_writes` is true.\n50\tpub const ORIGIN_ROLLOVER: &str = \"rollover\";\n51\t\n52\t/// Origin tag for TTL expiration deletes (plan §13.14).\n53\t/// These deletes are suppressed from CDC unless `emit_ttl_deletes` is true.\n54\tpub const ORIGIN_TTL_EXPIRE: &str = \"ttl_expire\";\n55\t\n56\t/// Callback type for incrementing the CDC events suppressed metric.\n57\t/// Called with the origin tag when an event is suppressed.\n58\tpub type CdcSuppressedMetricCallback = Arc<dyn Fn(&str) + Send + Sync>;\n59\t\n60\t/// CDC event published on every successful write (after quorum ACK).\n61\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n62\tpub struct CdcEvent {\n63\t    /// Miroir task ID.\n64\t    pub mtask_id:    pub timestamp: u64,\n77\t    /// Document body (optional, based on sink config).\n78\t    pub document: Option<serde_json::Value>,\n79\t    /// Internal origin tag (for suppressing internal Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n87\tpub enum CdcOperation {\n88\t    Add,\n89\t    Update,\n90\t    Delete,\n91\t}\n92\t\n93\t/// CDC sink configuration.\n94\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n95\tpub struct CdcSinkConfig {\n96\t    /// Sink type identifier.\n97\t    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n98\t    pub sink_type: CdcSinkType,\n99\t    /// Sink URL (for webhook/NATS/Kafka).\n100\t    pub url: String,\n101\t    /// Batch size for events.\n102\t    pub batch_size: u32,\n103\t    /// Batch flush interval (ms).\n104\t    pub batch_flush_ms: u64,\n105\t {\n116\t    Webhook,\n117\t    Nats,\n118\t    Kafka,\n119\t    Internal,\n120\t}\n121\t\n122\t/// CDC publisher state.\n123\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n124\tpub struct Deserialize)]\n177\tpub enum CdcBufferType {\n178\t    Memory,\n179\t    Redis,\n180\t    Pvc,\n181\t    Drop,\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\timpl Default for CdcConfig {\n185\t    fn default() -> Self {\n186\t        Self {\n187\t            enabled: true,\n188\t            emit_ttl_deletes: false,\n189\t            emit_internal_writes: false,\n190\t            sinks: Vec::new(),\n191\t            buffer:      redis_bytes: 1_073_741_824, // 1 GiB\n196\t            },\n197\t        }\n198\t    }\n199\t}\n200\t\n201\timpl CdcManager {\n202\t    /// Create a new CDC manager.\n203\t    pub fn new(config: CdcConfig) -> Self {\n204\t        Self::with_metrics(config, None)\n205\t    }\n206\t\n207\t    /// Create a new CDC manager with an optional suppression metric callback.\n208\t    pub fn with_metrics(config: CdcConfig, suppressed_metric_callback: Option<CdcSuppressedMetricCallback>) -> Self {\n209\t        let (event_tx, event_rx) = mpsc::unbounded_channel();\n210\t        let state = Arc::new(RwLock::new(CdcPublisherState {\n211\t            cursors: HashMap::new(),\n212\t            buffered_count: 0,\n213\t            dropped_count: 0,\n214\t            published_count: 0,\n215\t        }));\n216\t\n217\t        if config.enabled {\n218\t            // Spawn background publisher task\n219\t            let state_clone = state.clone();\n220\t            let config_clone = config.clone();\n221\t            tokio::spawn(async move {\n222\t                Self::background_publisher(event_rx, state_clone, config_clone).await;\n223\t            });\n224\t        }\n225\t\n226\t        Self {\n227\t            config,\n228\t            event_tx,\n229\t            state,\n230\t            suppressed_metric_callback,\n231\t        }\n232\t    }\n233\t\n234\t    /// Publish a CDC event (non-blocking).\n235\t    pub fn publish(&self, event: CdcEvent) -> Result<(), CdcError> {\n236\t        if !self.config.enabled {\n237\t            return Ok(());\n238\t        }\n239\t\n240\t        // Filter based on origin tag (plan §13.13: CDC event suppression)\n241\t        if let Some(ref origin) = event.origin {\n242\t            let should_suppress = match origin.as_str() {\n243\t                ORIGIN_ANTIENTROPY | ORIGIN_RESHARD_BACKFILL | ORIGIN_ROLLOVER => {\n244\t                    // Internal writes: suppressed unless emit_internal_writes is true\n245\t                    !self.config.emit_internal_writes\n246\t                }\n247\t                ORIGIN_TTL_EXPIRE => {\n248\t                    // TTL deletes: suppressed unless emit_ttl_deletes is true\n249\t                    !self.config.emit_ttl_deletes\n250\t                }\n251\t                _ => false,\n252\t            };\n253\t\n254\t            if should_suppress {\n255\t                debug!(\"CDC: suppressing event with origin {}\", origin);\n256\t                // Increment suppression metric if callback is provided\n257\t                if let Some(ref callback) = self.suppressed_metric_callback {\n258\t                    callback(origin);\n259\t                }\n260\t                return Ok(());\n261\t            }\n262\t        }\n263\t\n264\t        self.event_tx.send(event).map_err(|_| CdcError::ChannelClosed)?;\n265\t        Ok(())\n266\t    }\n267\t\n268\t    /// Get current publisher state.\n269\t    pub async fn state(&self) -> CdcPublisherState {\n270\t        self.state.read().await.clone()\n271\t    }\n272\t\n273\t    /// Background task that buffers and publishes events to sinks.\n274\t    async fn background_publisher(\n275\t        mut event_rx: mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<CdcEvent>,\n276\t        state: Arc<RwLock<CdcPublisherState>>,\n277\t        config: CdcConfig,\n278\t    ) {\n279\t        info!(\"CDC: background publisher started\");\n280\t\n281\t        // Per-sink event buffers\n282\t        let mut sink_buffers: HashMap<String, Vec<CdcEvent>> = HashMap::new();\n283\t\n284\t        while let Some(event) = event_rx.recv().await {\n285\t            // Buffer event for each sink\n286\t            for sink in &config.sinks {\n287\t                let buffer = sink_buffers.entry(sink.url.clone()).or_insert_with(Vec::new);\n288\t                buffer.push(event.clone());\n289\t\n290\t                // Flush if buffer size reached\n291\t                if buffer.len() >= sink.batch_size as usize {\n292\t                    if let Err(e) = Self::flush_sink(&sink, buffer, &state).await {\n293\t                        error!(\"CDC: failed to flush sink {}: {}\", sink.url, e);\n294\t                    }\n295\t                    sink_buffers.insert(sink.url.clone(), Vec::new());\n296\t                }\n297\t            }\n298\t        }\n299\t\n300\t        // Flush remaining buffers on shutdown\n301\t        for (sink_url, buffer) in sink_buffers {\n302\t            if !buffer.is_empty() {\n303\t                let sink = config.sinks.iter().find(|s| s.url == sink_url);\n304\t                if let Some(sink) = sink {\n305\t                    if let Err(e) = Self::flush_sink(sink, &buffer, &state).await {\n306\t                        error!(\"CDC: failed to flush sink {} on shutdown: {}\", sink_url, e);\n307\t                    }\n308\t                }\n309\t            }\n310\t        }\n311\t\n312\t        info!(\"CDC: background publisher stopped\");\n313\t    }\n314\t\n315\t    /// Flush buffered events to a single sink.\n316\t    async fn flush_sink(\n317\t        sink: &CdcSinkConfig,\n318\t        events: &[CdcEvent],\n319\t        _state: &Arc<RwLock<CdcPublisherState>>,\n320\t    ) -> Result<(), CdcError> {\n321\t        match sink.sink_type {\n322\t            CdcSinkType::Webhook => Self::flush_webhook(sink, events).await,\n323\t            CdcSinkType::Nats => Self::flush_nats(sink, events).await,\n324\t            CdcSinkType::Kafka => Self::flush_kafka(sink, events).await,\n325\t            CdcSinkType::Internal => {\n326\t                // Internal queue: events are stored in memory for polling\n327\t                // (implementation depends on internal queue design)\n328\t                Ok(())\n329\t            }\n330\t        }\n331\t    }\n332\t\n333\t    /// Flush events to a webhook sink.\n334\t    async fn flush_webhook(\n335\t        sink: &CdcSinkConfig,\n336\t        events: &[CdcEvent],\n337\t    ) -> Result<(), CdcError> {\n338\t        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n339\t        let response = client\n340\t            .post(&sink.url)\n341\t            .json(events)\n342\t            .send()\n343\t            .await\n344\t            .map_err(|e| CdcError::SinkError(e.to_string()))?;\n345\t\n346\t        if response.status().is_success() {\n347\t            Ok(())\n348\t        } else {\n349\t            let status = response.status();\n350\t            Err(CdcError::SinkError(format!(\"webhook returned {}\", status)))\n351\t        }\n352\t    }\n353\t\n354\t    /// Flush events to a NATS sink.\n355\t    async fn flush_nats(\n356\t        _sink: &CdcSinkConfig,\n357\t        _events: #[error(\"buffer overflow\")]\n383\t    BufferOverflow,\n384\t}\n385\t\n386\t#[cfg(test)]\n387\tmod tests {\n388\t    use super::*;\n389\t\n390\t    #[test]\n391\t    fn test_cdc_event_creation() test_cdc_suppression_metric_callback() {\n476\t        use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering};\n477\t\n478\t        let callback_called = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));\n479\t        let     callback_clone.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);\n484\t        });\n485\t\n486\t        let config = CdcConfig {\n487\t            enabled: true,\n488\t            emit_internal_writes:   mtask_id: \"mtask-123\".into(),\n495\t            index: \"products\".into(),\n496\t            operation: CdcOperation::Add,\n497\t            primary_keys: vec![\"sku-123\".into()],\n498\t CDC (Change Data Capture) — plan §13.13.\n//!\n//! Publishes document change events to configured sinks (webhook, NATS, Kafka, internal queue).\n//!\n//! # Origin Tag-Based Event Suppression\n//!\n//! The `_miroir_origin` tag is an **internal orchestrator-side marker** used to suppress\n//! internal write events from CDC by default. It is:\n//! - Never stored on documents\n//! - Never returned to clients\n//! - Never transmitted outside the orchestrator process\n//!\n//! ## Suppression Rules (plan §13.13)\n//!\n//! | Origin Tag | Write Type | Default Behavior | Opt-In Via |\n//! |------------|------------|-------------------|------------|\n//! | `None` | Client write | **Always emitted** | N/A |\n//! | `ANTIENTROPY` | Anti-entropy repair | Suppressed | `emit_internal_writes` |\n//! | `RESHARD_BACKFILL` | Reshard backfill | Suppressed | `emit_internal_writes` |\n//! | `ROLLOVER` | ILM rollover | Suppressed | `emit_internal_writes` |\n//! | `TTL_EXPIRE` | TTL expiration delete | Suppressed | `emit_ttl_deletes` |\n//!\n//! ## Usage\n//!\n//! Set the origin tag when constructing `WriteRequest`:\n//! ```ignore\n//! // Client write (always emitted to CDC)\n//! WriteRequest { origin: None, .. }\n//!\n//! // Anti-entropy repair write (suppressed by default)\n//! WriteRequest { origin: Some(ORIGIN_ANTIENTROPY.to_string()), .. }\n//! ```\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::{mpsc, RwLock};\nuse tracing::{debug, error, info};\n\n/// Origin tag for anti-entropy repair writes (plan §13.8).\n/// These writes are suppressed from CDC unless `emit_internal_writes` is true.\npub const ORIGIN_ANTIENTROPY: &str = \"antientropy\";\n\n/// Origin tag for reshard backfill writes (plan §13.1 steps 2-3).\n/// These writes are suppressed from CDC unless `emit_internal_writes` is true.\npub const ORIGIN_RESHARD_BACKFILL: &str = \"reshard_backfill\";\n\n/// Origin tag for ILM rollover writes (plan §13.17).\n/// These writes are suppressed from CDC unless `emit_internal_writes` is true.\npub const ORIGIN_ROLLOVER: &str = \"rollover\";\n\n/// Origin tag for TTL expiration deletes (plan §13.14).\n/// These deletes are suppressed from CDC unless `emit_ttl_deletes` is true.\npub const ORIGIN_TTL_EXPIRE: &str = \"ttl_expire\";\n\n/// Callback type for incrementing the CDC events suppressed metric.\n/// Called with the origin tag when an event is suppressed.\npub type CdcSuppressedMetricCallback = Arc<dyn Fn(&str) + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// CDC event published on every successful write (after quorum ACK).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CdcEvent {\n    /// Miroir task ID.\n    pub mtask_id: String,\n    /// Index UID.\n    pub index: String,\n    /// Operation type.\n    pub operation: CdcOperation,\n    /// Primary keys affected.\n    pub primary_keys: Vec<String>,\n    /// Shard IDs affected.\n    pub shard_ids: Vec<u32>,\n    /// Settings version at write time.\n    pub settings_version: u64,\n    /// UNIX timestamp (ms).\n    pub timestamp: u64,\n    /// Document body (optional, based on sink config).\n    pub document: Option<serde_json::Value>,\n    /// Internal origin tag (for suppressing internal writes).\n    pub origin: Option<String>,\n    /// Stable event ID for deduplication.\n    pub event_id: String,\n}\n\n/// Operation type for a CDC event.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum CdcOperation {\n    Add,\n    Update,\n    Delete,\n}\n\n/// CDC sink configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CdcSinkConfig {\n    /// Sink type identifier.\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub sink_type: CdcSinkType,\n    /// Sink URL (for webhook/NATS/Kafka).\n    pub url: String,\n    /// Batch size for events.\n    pub batch_size: u32,\n    /// Batch flush interval (ms).\n    pub batch_flush_ms: u64,\n    /// Whether to include document body.\n    pub include_body: bool,\n    /// Maximum retry time (seconds).\n    pub retry_max_s: u64,\n    /// NATS subject prefix (for NATS sinks).\n    pub subject_prefix: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Sink type variants.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum CdcSinkType {\n    Webhook,\n    Nats,\n    Kafka,\n    Internal,\n}\n\n/// CDC publisher state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CdcPublisherState {\n    /// Per-sink cursor (sequence number).\n    pub cursors: HashMap<String, u64>,\n    /// Buffered event count.\n    pub buffered_count: usize,\n    /// Dropped event count.\n    pub dropped_count: u64,\n    /// Total published count.\n    pub published_count: u64,\n}\n\n/// CDC manager — publishes change events to configured sinks.\npub struct CdcManager {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: CdcConfig,\n    /// Event sender channel.\n    event_tx: mpsc::UnboundedSender<CdcEvent>,\n    /// Per-sink state (shared with background task).\n    state: Arc<RwLock<CdcPublisherState>>,\n    /// Optional callback to increment suppression metric.\n    suppressed_metric_callback: Option<CdcSuppressedMetricCallback>,\n}\n\n/// CDC manager configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CdcConfig {\n    /// Whether CDC is enabled.\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    /// Whether to emit TTL delete events.\n    pub emit_ttl_deletes: bool,\n    /// Whether to emit internal writes (debug only).\n    pub emit_internal_writes: bool,\n    /// Configured sinks.\n    pub sinks: Vec<CdcSinkConfig>,\n    /// Buffer configuration.\n    pub buffer: CdcBufferConfig,\n}\n\n/// Buffer configuration for CDC events.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CdcBufferConfig {\n    /// Primary buffer type.\n    pub primary: CdcBufferType,\n    /// In-memory buffer size (bytes).\n    pub memory_bytes: u64,\n    /// Overflow buffer type.\n    pub overflow: CdcBufferType,\n    /// Redis overflow size (bytes).\n    pub redis_bytes: u64,\n}\n\n/// Buffer type variants.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum CdcBufferType {\n    Memory,\n    Redis,\n    Pvc,\n    Drop,\n}\n\nimpl Default for CdcConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            emit_ttl_deletes: false,\n            emit_internal_writes: false,\n            sinks: Vec::new(),\n            buffer: CdcBufferConfig {\n                primary: CdcBufferType::Memory,\n                memory_bytes: 67_108_864, // 64 MiB\n                overflow: CdcBufferType::Redis,\n                redis_bytes: 1_073_741_824, // 1 GiB\n            },\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl CdcManager {\n    /// Create a new CDC manager.\n    pub fn new(config: CdcConfig) -> Self {\n        Self::with_metrics(config, None)\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new CDC manager with an optional suppression metric callback.\n    pub fn with_metrics(config: CdcConfig, suppressed_metric_callback: Option<CdcSuppressedMetricCallback>) -> Self {\n        let (event_tx, event_rx) = mpsc::unbounded_channel();\n        let state = Arc::new(RwLock::new(CdcPublisherState {\n            cursors: HashMap::new(),\n            buffered_count: 0,\n            dropped_count: 0,\n            published_count: 0,\n        }));\n\n        if config.enabled {\n            // Spawn background publisher task\n            let state_clone = state.clone();\n            let config_clone = config.clone();\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                Self::background_publisher(event_rx, state_clone, config_clone).await;\n            });\n        }\n\n        Self {\n            config,\n            event_tx,\n            state,\n            suppressed_metric_callback,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Publish a CDC event (non-blocking).\n    pub fn publish(&self, event: CdcEvent) -> Result<(), CdcError> {\n        if !self.config.enabled {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        // Filter based on origin tag (plan §13.13: CDC event suppression)\n        if let Some(ref origin) = event.origin {\n            let should_suppress = match origin.as_str() {\n                ORIGIN_ANTIENTROPY | ORIGIN_RESHARD_BACKFILL | ORIGIN_ROLLOVER => {\n                    // Internal writes: suppressed unless emit_internal_writes is true\n                    !self.config.emit_internal_writes\n                }\n                ORIGIN_TTL_EXPIRE => {\n                    // TTL deletes: suppressed unless emit_ttl_deletes is true\n                    !self.config.emit_ttl_deletes\n                }\n                _ => false,\n            };\n\n            if should_suppress {\n                debug!(\"CDC: suppressing event with origin {}\", origin);\n                // Increment suppression metric if callback is provided\n                if let Some(ref callback) = self.suppressed_metric_callback {\n                    callback(origin);\n                }\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.event_tx.send(event).map_err(|_| CdcError::ChannelClosed)?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get current publisher state.\n    pub async fn state(&self) -> CdcPublisherState {\n        self.state.read().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Background task that buffers and publishes events to sinks.\n    async fn background_publisher(\n        mut event_rx: mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<CdcEvent>,\n        state: Arc<RwLock<CdcPublisherState>>,\n        config: CdcConfig,\n    ) {\n        info!(\"CDC: background publisher started\");\n\n        // Per-sink event buffers\n        let mut sink_buffers: HashMap<String, Vec<CdcEvent>> = HashMap::new();\n\n        while let Some(event) = event_rx.recv().await {\n            // Buffer event for each sink\n            for sink in &config.sinks {\n                let buffer = sink_buffers.entry(sink.url.clone()).or_insert_with(Vec::new);\n                buffer.push(event.clone());\n\n                // Flush if buffer size reached\n                if buffer.len() >= sink.batch_size as usize {\n                    if let Err(e) = Self::flush_sink(&sink, buffer, &state).await {\n                        error!(\"CDC: failed to flush sink {}: {}\", sink.url, e);\n                    }\n                    sink_buffers.insert(sink.url.clone(), Vec::new());\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Flush remaining buffers on shutdown\n        for (sink_url, buffer) in sink_buffers {\n            if !buffer.is_empty() {\n                let sink = config.sinks.iter().find(|s| s.url == sink_url);\n                if let Some(sink) = sink {\n                    if let Err(e) = Self::flush_sink(sink, &buffer, &state).await {\n                        error!(\"CDC: failed to flush sink {} on shutdown: {}\", sink_url, e);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        info!(\"CDC: background publisher stopped\");\n    }\n\n    /// Flush buffered events to a single sink.\n    async fn flush_sink(\n        sink: &CdcSinkConfig,\n        events: &[CdcEvent],\n        _state: &Arc<RwLock<CdcPublisherState>>,\n    ) -> Result<(), CdcError> {\n        match sink.sink_type {\n            CdcSinkType::Webhook => Self::flush_webhook(sink, events).await,\n            CdcSinkType::Nats => Self::flush_nats(sink, events).await,\n            CdcSinkType::Kafka => Self::flush_kafka(sink, events).await,\n            CdcSinkType::Internal => {\n                // Internal queue: events are stored in memory for polling\n                // (implementation depends on internal queue design)\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Flush events to a webhook sink.\n    async fn flush_webhook(\n        sink: &CdcSinkConfig,\n        events: &[CdcEvent],\n    ) -> Result<(), CdcError> {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        let response = client\n            .post(&sink.url)\n            .json(events)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| CdcError::SinkError(e.to_string()))?;\n\n        if response.status().is_success() {\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            let status = response.status();\n            Err(CdcError::SinkError(format!(\"webhook returned {}\", status)))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Flush events to a NATS sink.\n    async fn flush_nats(\n        _sink: &CdcSinkConfig,\n        _events: &[CdcEvent],\n    ) -> Result<(), CdcError> {\n        // NATS publishing implementation\n        // (requires async-nats crate)\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Flush events to a Kafka sink.\n    async fn flush_kafka(\n        _sink: &CdcSinkConfig,\n        _events: &[CdcEvent],\n    ) -> Result<(), CdcError> {\n        // Kafka publishing implementation\n        // (requires rustafka or rdkafka crate)\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// CDC error types.\n#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]\npub enum CdcError {\n    #[error(\"channel closed\")]\n    ChannelClosed,\n    #[error(\"sink error: {0}\")]\n    SinkError(String),\n    #[error(\"buffer overflow\")]\n    BufferOverflow,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_cdc_event_creation() {\n        let event = CdcEvent {\n            mtask_id: \"mtask-123\".into(),\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            operation: CdcOperation::Add,\n            primary_keys: vec![\"sku-123\".into()],\n            shard_ids: vec![5],\n            settings_version: 1,\n            timestamp: 1234567890,\n            document: None,\n            origin: None,\n            event_id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string(),\n        };\n        assert_eq!(event.index, \"products\");\n        assert_eq!(event.operation, CdcOperation::Add);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_cdc_config_default() {\n        let config = CdcConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.enabled);\n        assert!(!config.emit_ttl_deletes);\n        assert!(!config.emit_internal_writes);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cdc_manager_publish() {\n        let config = CdcConfig {\n            enabled: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let manager =            event_id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string(),\n        };\n\n        // Should not error\n        assert!(manager.publish(event).is_ok());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn Some(\"antientropy\".into()),\n            event_id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string(),\n        };\n\n        assert!(manager.publish(event).is_ok());\n        assert_eq!(callback_called.load(Ordering::SeqCst), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cdc_suppression_metric_all_origins() {\n        use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering};\n        use std::collections::HashSet;\n\n        let suppressed_origins = Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(HashSet::new()));\n        let origins_clone = suppressed_origins.clone();\n\n        let     let event = CdcEvent {\n                mtask_id: \"mtask-123\".into(),\n                index: \"products\".into(),\n                operation: CdcOperation::Delete,\n      assert!(suppressed.contains(\"rollover\"));\n        assert!(suppressed.contains(\"ttl_expire\"));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cdc_no_suppression_with_emit_internal_writes() {\n        use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering};\n\n        let callback_called = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));\n        let callback_clone = callback_called.clone();\n\n        let callback: CdcSuppressedMetricCallback = Arc::new(move |_origin| {\n            callback_clone.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);\n        });\n\n        let config = CdcConfig {\n            enabled: result.\n77\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n78\tpub struct ReplicaDiff {\n79\t    /// Shard ID.\n80\t    pub shard_id: u32,\n81\t    /// Primary keys only in replica A.\n82\t    pub a_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n83\t    /// Primary keys only in replica B.\n84\t    pub b_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n85\t    /// Primary keys with content hash mismatch.\n86\t    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\t/// Repair action.\n90\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n91\tpub struct RepairAction {\n92\t    /// Shard ID.\n93\t    pub shard_id: u32,\n94\t    /// Primary key to repair.\n95\t    pub primary_key: String,\n96\t    /// Authoritative version (document JSON).\n97\t    pub authoritative_doc: serde_json::Value,\n98\t    /// Target nodes that need repair.\n99\t    pub target_nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n100\t    /// Reason for repair.\n101\t    pub reason: RepairReason,\n102\t}\n103\t\n104\t/// Why a repair is needed.\n105\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n106\tpub enum RepairReason {\n107\t    /// Document missing on replica.\n108\t    Missing,\n109\t    /// Content hash mismatch.\n110\t    Mismatch,\n111\t    /// Expired document resurrection (TTL interaction).\n112\t    ExpiredResurrection,\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t/// Reconciler pass result.\n116\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n117\tpub struct ReconcilerPass {\n118\t    /// Pass started at (UNIX ms).\n119\t    pub started_at: u64,\n120\t    /// Pass completed at (UNIX ms).\n121\t    pub completed_at: u64,\n122\t    /// Shards scanned.\n123\t    pub shards_scanned: u32,\n124\t    /// Shards with drift detected.\n125\t    pub shards_with_drift: u32,\n126\t    /// Repairs performed.\n127\t    pub repairs_performed: u32,\n128\t    /// Errors encountered.\n129\t    pub errors: Vec<String>,\n130\t}\n131\t\n132\t/// Anti-entropy reconciler.\n133\tpub struct AntiEntropyReconciler<C: NodeClient> {\n134\t    /// Configuration.\n135\t    config: AntiEntropyConfig,\n136\t    /// Shared topology.\n137\t    topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n138\t    /// Pass history.\n139\t    pass_history: Arc<RwLock<Vec<ReconcilerPass>>>,\n140\t    /// Currently running pass.\n141\t    current_pass: Arc<RwLock<Option<ReconcilerPass>>>,\n142\t    /// HTTP client for node communication.\n143\t    node_client: Arc<C>,\n144\t}\n145\t\n146\timpl<C: NodeClient> AntiEntropyReconciler<C> {\n147\t    /// Create a new anti-entropy reconciler.\n148\t    pub fn new(\n149\t        config: AntiEntropyConfig,\n150\t        topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n151\t        node_client: Arc<C>,\n152\t    ) -> Self {\n153\t        Self {\n154\t            config,\n155\t            topology,\n156\t            pass_history: Arc::new(RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n157\t            current_pass: Arc::new(RwLock::new(None)),\n158\t            node_client,\n159\t        }\n160\t    }\n161\t\n162\t    /// Compute the canonical content hash of a document.\n163\t    ///\n164\t    /// The canonical form excludes internal Miroir fields (_miroir_*, _rankingScore)\n165\t    /// and serializes with sorted keys for deterministic hashing.\n166\t    pub fn compute_content_hash(document: &Value) -> u64 {\n167\t        // Remove internal fields to get canonical content\n168\t        let mut canonical = document.clone();\n169\t        if let Some(obj) = canonical.as_object_mut() {\n170\t            // Remove all _miroir_* fields\n171\t            obj.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n172\t            // Remove _rankingScore (not content, used for scoring)\n173\t            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n174\t        }\n175\t\n176\t        // Serialize with sorted keys for deterministic output\n177\t        let canonical_json = if let Some(obj) = canonical.as_object() {\n178\t            // Use BTreeMap to sort keys\n179\t            let sorted: BTreeMap<_, _> = obj.iter().collect();\n180\t            serde_json::to_string(&sorted).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"{}\".to_string())\n181\t        } else {\n182\t            serde_json::to_string(&canonical).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"{}\".to_string())\n183\t        };\n184\t\n185\t        // Hash using xxh3 (xxhash family, same as router)\n186\t        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n187\t        hasher.write(canonical_json.as_bytes());\n188\t        hasher.finish()\n189\t    }\n190\t\n191\t    /// Fingerprint a single shard on a node (plan §13.8 step 1).\n192\t    ///\n193\t    /// Iterates all documents with filter=_miroir_shard={id}, computes\n194\t    /// hash(primary_key || content_hash) for each, and folds into a\n195\t    /// streaming xxh3 digest.\n196\t    pub async fn fingerprint_shard(\n197\t        &self,\n198\t        node_id: &NodeId,\n199\t        shard_id: u32,\n200\t        index_uid: &str,\n201\t        address: &str,\n202\t    ) -> Result<ShardFingerprint> {\n203\t        let batch_size = self.config.fingerprint_batch_size as usize;\n204\t        let mut offset = 0u32;\n205\t        let mut document_count = 0u64;\n206\t        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(shard_id as u64); // Shard-seeded digest\n207\t\n208\t        // Paginated iteration through documents\n209\t        loop {\n210\t            let filter = serde_json::json!({ \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id });\n211\t            let request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n212\t                index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n213\t                filter,\n214\t                limit: batch_size as u32,\n215\t                offset,\n216\t            };\n217\t\n218\t            let response: FetchDocumentsResponse = self\n219\t                .node_client\n220\t                .fetch_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n221\t                .await\n222\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"fetch failed: {:?}\", e)))?;\n223\t\n224\t            if response.results.is_empty() {\n225\t                break; // No more documents\n226\t            }\n227\t\n228\t            for doc in &response.results {\n229\t                // Extract primary key\n230\t                let primary_key = doc\n231\t                    .get(\"id\")\n232\t                    .or(doc.get(\"_id\"))\n233\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n234\t                    .unwrap_or(\"\");\n235\t\n236\t                // Compute content hash\n237\t                let content_hash = Self::compute_content_hash(doc);\n238\t\n239\t                // Fold: hash(primary_key || content_hash) into digest\n240\t                let mut pk_hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n241\t                pk_hasher.write(primary_key.as_bytes());\n242\t                pk_hasher.write_u64(content_hash);\n243\t                let doc_hash = pk_hasher.finish();\n244\t\n245\t                // Fold into shard digest\n246\t                hasher.write_u64(doc_hash);\n247\t                document_count += 1;\n248\t            }\n249\t\n250\t            offset += batch_size as u32;\n251\t\n252\t            // Self-throttle: small sleep between batches to target <2% CPU\n253\t            // (In production, this would be adaptive based on CPU metrics)\n254\t            tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n255\t        }\n256\t\n257\t        let merkle_root = format!(\"xxh3:{}\", hasher.finish());\n258\t\n259\t        debug!(\n260\t            \"Fingerprinted shard {} on node {}: {} documents, root {}\",\n261\t            shard_id, node_id, document_count, merkle_root\n262\t        );\n263\t\n264\t        Ok(ShardFingerprint {\n265\t            shard_id,\n266\t            node_id: node_id.to_string(),\n267\t            merkle_root,\n268\t            document_count,\n269\t            bucket_hashes: Vec::new(), // Computed on-demand during diff\n270\t        })\n271\t    }\n272\t\n273\t    /// Run a single reconciliation pass.\n274\t    pub async fn run_pass(&self) -> Result<ReconcilerPass> {\n275\t        let mut pass = ReconcilerPass {\n276\t            started_at: millis_now(),\n277\t            completed_at: 0,\n278\t            shards_scanned: 0,\n279\t            shards_with_drift: 0,\n280\t            repairs_performed: 0,\n281\t            errors: Vec::new(),\n282\t        };\n283\t\n284\t        // Set as current pass\n285\t        {\n286\t            let mut current = self.current_pass.write().await;\n287\t            *current = Some(pass.clone());\n288\t        }\n289\t\n290\t        let topology = self.topology.read().await;\n291\t        let shard_count = topology.shards;\n292\t        let replica_groups = topology.groups().count() as u32;\n293\t\n294\t        // Determine which shards to scan\n295\t        let shards_to_scan = if self.config.shards_per_pass == 0 {\n296\t            // Scan all shards\n297\t            (0..shard_count).collect::<Vec<_>>()\n298\t        } else {\n299\t            // Scan a subset (for throttling)\n300\t            (0..shard_count)\n301\t                .take(self.config.shards_per_pass as usize)\n302\t                .collect()\n303\t        };\n304\t\n305\t        info!(\n306\t            \"Anti-entropy pass starting: {} shards to scan\",\n307\t            shards_to_scan.len()\n308\t        );\n309\t\n310\t        // Scan each shard\n311\t        for shard_id in shards_to_scan {\n312\t            match self.scan_shard(&topology, shard_id).await {\n313\t                Ok(drift_detected) => {\n314\t                    pass.shards_scanned += 1;\n315\t                    if drift_detected {\n316\t                        pass.shards_with_drift += 1;\n317\t                    }\n318\t                }\n319\t                Err(e) => {\n320\t                    pass.errors.push(format!(\"shard {}: {}\", shard_id, e));\n321\t                }\n322\t            }\n323\t        }\n324\t\n325\t        pass.completed_at = millis_now();\n326\t\n327\t        // Archive pass\n328\t        {\n329\t            let mut history = self.pass_history.write().await;\n330\t            history.push(pass.clone());\n331\t            // Keep last 100 passes\n332\t            if history.len() > 100 {\n333\t                history.remove(0);\n334\t            }\n335\t        }\n336\t\n337\t        // Clear current pass\n338\t        {\n339\t            let mut current = self.current_pass.write().await;\n340\t            *current = None;\n341\t        }\n342\t\n343\t        info!(\n344\t            \"Anti-entropy pass completed: {} shards scanned, {} with drift, {} repairs\",\n345\t            pass.shards_scanned, pass.shards_with_drift, pass.repairs_performed\n346\t        );\n347\t\n348\t        Ok(pass)\n349\t    }\n350\t\n351\t    /// Scan a single shard for drift.\n352\t    async fn scan_shard(&self, topology: &Topology, shard_id: u32) -> Result<bool> {\n353\t        // For each replica group, get the assigned nodes\n354\t        let mut fingerprints = Vec::new();\n355\t\n356\t        for group in topology.groups() {\n357\t            let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n358\t            for node_id in assigned {\n359\t                // Look up node address from topology\n360\t                let topology_guard = self.topology.read().await;\n361\t                let node = topology_guard\n362\t                    .node(&node_id)\n363\t                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"node {} not found\", node_id)))?;\n364\t\n365\t                if !node.is_healthy() {\n366\t                    warn!(\"Node {} is not healthy, skipping fingerprint\", node_id);\n367\t                    continue;\n368\t                }\n369\t\n370\t                let address = node.address.clone();\n371\t                drop(topology_guard);\n372\t\n373\t                match self\n374\t                    .fingerprint_shard(&node_id, shard_id, &self.config.index_uid, &address)\n375\t                    .await\n376\t                {\n377\t                    Ok(fp) => fingerprints.push((node_id, fp)),\n378\t                    Err(e) => {\n379\t                        warn!(\n380\t                            \"Failed to fingerprint shard {} on node {}: {}\",\n381\t                            shard_id, node_id, e\n382\t                        );\n383\t                        // Continue with other nodes\n384\t                    }\n385\t                }\n386\t            }\n387\t        }\n388\t\n389\t        if fingerprints.is_empty() {\n390\t            // No readable replicas\n391\t            return Ok(false);\n392\t        }\n393\t\n394\t        // Compare fingerprints\n395\t        let reference = &fingerprints[0].1;\n396\t        let mut drift_detected = false;\n397\t\n398\t        for (node_id, fp) in &fingerprints[1..] {\n399\t            if fp.merkle_root != reference.merkle_root {\n400\t                drift_detected = true;\n401\t                debug!(\n402\t                    \"Shard {} drift detected: node {} has different merkle root\",\n403\t                    shard_id, node_id\n404\t                );\n405\t\n406\t                if self.config.auto_repair {\n407\t                    // Perform detailed diff and repair\n408\t                    if let Err(e) = self.repair_shard(shard_id, reference, fp).await {\n409\t                        error!(\n410\t                            \"Failed to repair shard {} on node {}: {}\",\n411\t                            shard_id, node_id, e\n412\t                        );\n413\t                    }\n414\t                }\n415\t            }\n416\t        }\n417\t\n418\t        Ok(drift_detected)\n419\t    }\n420\t\n421\t    /// Repair a shard by comparing replicas and applying fixes.\n422\t    async fn repair_shard(\n423\t        &self,\n424\t        shard_id: u32,\n425\t        reference: &ShardFingerprint,\n426\t        target: &ShardFingerprint,\n427\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n428\t        // In a real implementation, this would:\n429\t        // 1. Compute per-bucket hashes to locate divergent documents\n430\t        // 2. Enumerate divergent primary keys\n431\t        // 3. For each divergent PK:\n432\t        //    a. Check if any replica has _miroir_expires_at <= now (TTL interaction)\n433\t        //    b. If expired: delete from all replicas\n434\t        //    c. Otherwise: pick authoritative version (highest _miroir_updated_at)\n435\t        //    d. Write authoritative version to divergent replicas\n436\t\n437\t        debug!(\n438\t            \"Repairing shard {} on node {}\",\n439\t            shard_id, target.node_id\n440\t        );\n441\t\n442\t        Ok(())\n443\t    }\n444\t\n445\t    /// Get pass history.\n446\t    pub async fn pass_history(&self) -> Vec<ReconcilerPass> {\n447\t        self.pass_history.read().await.clone()\n448\t    }\n449\t\n450\t    /// Get current pass in progress.\n451\t    pub async fn current_pass(&self) -> Option<ReconcilerPass> {\n452\t        self.current_pass.read().await.clone()\n453\t    }\n454\t}\n455\t\n456\t/// Validates that migration is safe given the anti-entropy configuration.\n457\t/// Returns Ok(()) if safe, Err with a descriptive message if not.\n458\t///\n459\t/// Hard refusal policy (plan §15 OP#1): skipping the delta pass while\n460\t/// anti-entropy is disabled provides zero recovery path for documents\n461\t/// written at the cutover boundary. Measured loss rate: ~2% of writes.\n462\t/// This is a hard-coded policy, not a warning.\n463\tpub fn validate_migration_safety(\n464\t    ae_config: &AntiEntropyConfig,\n465\t    migration_config: &MigrationConfig,\n466\t) -> std::result::Result<(), MigrationError> {\n467\t    if migration_config.skip_delta_pass && !ae_config.enabled {\n468\t        return Err(MigrationError::UnsafeCutoverNoAntiEntropy);\n469\t    }\n470\t    Ok(())\n471\t}\n472\t\n473\t/// Generates a warning if anti-entropy is disabled during active migration.\n474\t/// The caller should log this at warn level.\n475\t///\n476\t/// Even with the delta pass enabled (which provides 0-loss cutover on its own),\n477\t/// disabling anti-entropy means the delta pass is the sole safety mechanism.\n478\t/// Operators should be aware of this reduced redundancy.\n479\tpub fn migration_warning_if_ae_disabled(ae_enabled: bool) -> Option<String> {\n480\t    if ae_enabled {\n481\t        return None;\n482\t    }\n483\t    Some(\n484\t        \"Anti-entropy is disabled. Shard migration cutover relies on the delta pass \\\n485\t         as the sole safety mechanism. Any bugs in the delta pass could lead to \\\n486\t         data loss at the cutover boundary. Re-enable anti-entropy for defense-in-depth.\"\n487\t            .to_string(),\n488\t    )\n489\t}\n490\t\n491\t/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\n492\tfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n493\t    SystemTime::now()\n494\t        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n495\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n496\t        .as_millis() as u64\n497\t}\n498\t\n499\t#[cfg(test)]\n500\tmod tests {\n501\t    use }\n593\t\n594\t    #[test]\n595\t    fn test_compute_content_hash_different_content() {\n596\t        let doc1 = json!({\n597\t            \"id\": \"test-3\",\n598\t            \"title\": }\n611\t\n612\t    #[test]\n613\t    fn test_compute_content_hash_key_order_independence() {\n614\t        // JSON objects with same fields but different key order\n615\t        let doc1 = }\n634\t}\n635\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"fc238a5e-fe53-4c2d-a2c4-570fda1f6acb","uuid":"4d9115b0-1c22-454f-800c-d8c09346c3bc","timestamp":"2026-05-23T12:28:06.246Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/anti_entropy.rs","content":"//! Anti-entropy reconciler module (plan §13.8).\n//!\n//! Detects and repairs replica drift using the fingerprint → diff → repair pipeline.\n//! Resolves Open Problem #1 (dual-write safety) by continuously reconciling\n//! replicas and catching any missed writes.\n//!\n//! # CDC Origin Tag (plan §13.13)\n//!\n//! Repair writes must be tagged with `origin=\"antientropy\"` so they are suppressed\n//! from CDC by default (unless `emit_internal_writes` is true).\n//!\n//! When constructing `WriteRequest` for repair writes, set:\n//! ```ignore\n//! use miroir_core::cdc::ORIGIN_ANTIENTROPY;\n//! WriteRequest { ..., origin: Some(ORIGIN_ANTIENTROPY.to_string()) }\n//! ```\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::migration::{MigrationConfig, MigrationError};\nuse crate::router::assign_shard_in_group;\nuse crate::scatter::{FetchDocumentsRequest, FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeClient};\nuse crate::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::{json, Value};\nuse std::collections::BTreeMap;\nuse std::hash::Hasher;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{debug, error, info, warn};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n\n/// Anti-entropy configuration (plan §13.8).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AntiEntropyConfig {\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub schedule: String,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub shards_per_pass: u32,\n    pub max_read_concurrency: u32,\n    pub fingerprint_batch_size: u32,\n    pub auto_repair: bool,\n    pub updated_at_field: String,\n}\n\nimpl Default for AntiEntropyConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            schedule: \"every 6h\".to_string(),\n            index_uid: \"default\".to_string(),\n            shards_per_pass: 0,\n            max_read_concurrency: 2,\n            fingerprint_batch_size: 1000,\n            auto_repair: true,\n            updated_at_field: \"_miroir_updated_at\".to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Shard fingerprint for comparison.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ShardFingerprint {\n    /// Shard ID.\n    pub shard_id: u32,\n    /// Node ID.\n    pub node_id: String,\n    /// Merkle root of document hashes.\n    pub merkle_root: String,\n    /// Document count.\n    pub document_count: u64,\n    /// Per-bucket hashes for detailed diff.\n    pub bucket_hashes: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n/// Replica diff result.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReplicaDiff {\n    /// Shard ID.\n    pub shard_id: u32,\n    /// Primary keys only in replica A.\n    pub a_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// Primary keys only in replica B.\n    pub b_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// Primary keys with content hash mismatch.\n    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n/// Repair action.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RepairAction {\n    /// Shard ID.\n    pub shard_id: u32,\n    /// Primary key to repair.\n    pub primary_key: String,\n    /// Authoritative version (document JSON).\n    pub authoritative_doc: serde_json::Value,\n    /// Target nodes that need repair.\n    pub target_nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n    /// Reason for repair.\n    pub reason: RepairReason,\n}\n\n/// Why a repair is needed.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum RepairReason {\n    /// Document missing on replica.\n    Missing,\n    /// Content hash mismatch.\n    Mismatch,\n    /// Expired document resurrection (TTL interaction).\n    ExpiredResurrection,\n}\n\n/// Reconciler pass result.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReconcilerPass {\n    /// Pass started at (UNIX ms).\n    pub started_at: u64,\n    /// Pass completed at (UNIX ms).\n    pub completed_at: u64,\n    /// Shards scanned.\n    pub shards_scanned: u32,\n    /// Shards with drift detected.\n    pub shards_with_drift: u32,\n    /// Repairs performed.\n    pub repairs_performed: u32,\n    /// Errors encountered.\n    pub errors: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n/// Anti-entropy reconciler.\npub struct AntiEntropyReconciler<C: NodeClient> {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: AntiEntropyConfig,\n    /// Shared topology.\n    topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n    /// Pass history.\n    pass_history: Arc<RwLock<Vec<ReconcilerPass>>>,\n    /// Currently running pass.\n    current_pass: Arc<RwLock<Option<ReconcilerPass>>>,\n    /// HTTP client for node communication.\n    node_client: Arc<C>,\n}\n\nimpl<C: NodeClient> AntiEntropyReconciler<C> {\n    /// Create a new anti-entropy reconciler.\n    pub fn new(\n        config: AntiEntropyConfig,\n        topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n        node_client: Arc<C>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            topology,\n            pass_history: Arc::new(RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n            current_pass: Arc::new(RwLock::new(None)),\n            node_client,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Compute the canonical content hash of a document.\n    ///\n    /// The canonical form excludes internal Miroir fields (_miroir_*, _rankingScore)\n    /// and serializes with sorted keys for deterministic hashing.\n    pub fn compute_content_hash(document: &Value) -> u64 {\n        // Remove internal fields to get canonical content\n        let mut canonical = document.clone();\n        if let Some(obj) = canonical.as_object_mut() {\n            // Remove all _miroir_* fields\n            obj.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n            // Remove _rankingScore (not content, used for scoring)\n            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n        }\n\n        // Serialize with sorted keys for deterministic output\n        let canonical_json = if let Some(obj) = canonical.as_object() {\n            // Use BTreeMap to sort keys\n            let sorted: BTreeMap<_, _> = obj.iter().collect();\n            serde_json::to_string(&sorted).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"{}\".to_string())\n        } else {\n            serde_json::to_string(&canonical).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"{}\".to_string())\n        };\n\n        // Hash using xxh3 (xxhash family, same as router)\n        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n        hasher.write(canonical_json.as_bytes());\n        hasher.finish()\n    }\n\n    /// Fingerprint a single shard on a node (plan §13.8 step 1).\n    ///\n    /// Iterates all documents with filter=_miroir_shard={id}, computes\n    /// hash(primary_key || content_hash) for each, and folds into a\n    /// streaming xxh3 digest.\n    pub async fn fingerprint_shard(\n        &self,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        shard_id: u32,\n        index_uid: &str,\n        address: &str,\n    ) -> Result<ShardFingerprint> {\n        let batch_size = self.config.fingerprint_batch_size as usize;\n        let mut offset = 0u32;\n        let mut document_count = 0u64;\n        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(shard_id as u64); // Shard-seeded digest\n\n        // Paginated iteration through documents\n        loop {\n            let filter = serde_json::json!({ \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id });\n            let request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n                filter,\n                limit: batch_size as u32,\n                offset,\n            };\n\n            let response: FetchDocumentsResponse = self\n                .node_client\n                .fetch_documents(node_id, address, &request)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"fetch failed: {:?}\", e)))?;\n\n            if response.results.is_empty() {\n                break; // No more documents\n            }\n\n            for doc in &response.results {\n                // Extract primary key\n                let primary_key = doc\n                    .get(\"id\")\n                    .or(doc.get(\"_id\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .unwrap_or(\"\");\n\n                // Compute content hash\n                let content_hash = Self::compute_content_hash(doc);\n\n                // Fold: hash(primary_key || content_hash) into digest\n                let mut pk_hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n                pk_hasher.write(primary_key.as_bytes());\n                pk_hasher.write_u64(content_hash);\n                let doc_hash = pk_hasher.finish();\n\n                // Fold into shard digest\n                hasher.write_u64(doc_hash);\n                document_count += 1;\n            }\n\n            offset += batch_size as u32;\n\n            // Self-throttle: small sleep between batches to target <2% CPU\n            // (In production, this would be adaptive based on CPU metrics)\n            tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n        }\n\n        let merkle_root = format!(\"xxh3:{}\", hasher.finish());\n\n        debug!(\n            \"Fingerprinted shard {} on node {}: {} documents, root {}\",\n            shard_id, node_id, document_count, merkle_root\n        );\n\n        Ok(ShardFingerprint {\n            shard_id,\n            node_id: node_id.to_string(),\n            merkle_root,\n            document_count,\n            bucket_hashes: Vec::new(), // Computed on-demand during diff\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Run a single reconciliation pass.\n    pub async fn run_pass(&self) -> Result<ReconcilerPass> {\n        let mut pass = ReconcilerPass {\n            started_at: millis_now(),\n            completed_at: 0,\n            shards_scanned: 0,\n            shards_with_drift: 0,\n            repairs_performed: 0,\n            errors: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        // Set as current pass\n        {\n            let mut current = self.current_pass.write().await;\n            *current = Some(pass.clone());\n        }\n\n        let topology = self.topology.read().await;\n        let shard_count = topology.shards;\n        let replica_groups = topology.groups().count() as u32;\n\n        // Determine which shards to scan\n        let shards_to_scan = if self.config.shards_per_pass == 0 {\n            // Scan all shards\n            (0..shard_count).collect::<Vec<_>>()\n        } else {\n            // Scan a subset (for throttling)\n            (0..shard_count)\n                .take(self.config.shards_per_pass as usize)\n                .collect()\n        };\n\n        info!(\n            \"Anti-entropy pass starting: {} shards to scan\",\n            shards_to_scan.len()\n        );\n\n        // Scan each shard\n        for shard_id in shards_to_scan {\n            match self.scan_shard(&topology, shard_id).await {\n                Ok(drift_detected) => {\n                    pass.shards_scanned += 1;\n                    if drift_detected {\n                        pass.shards_with_drift += 1;\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    pass.errors.push(format!(\"shard {}: {}\", shard_id, e));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        pass.completed_at = millis_now();\n\n        // Archive pass\n        {\n            let mut history = self.pass_history.write().await;\n            history.push(pass.clone());\n            // Keep last 100 passes\n            if history.len() > 100 {\n                history.remove(0);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Clear current pass\n        {\n            let mut current = self.current_pass.write().await;\n            *current = None;\n        }\n\n        info!(\n            \"Anti-entropy pass completed: {} shards scanned, {} with drift, {} repairs\",\n            pass.shards_scanned, pass.shards_with_drift, pass.repairs_performed\n        );\n\n        Ok(pass)\n    }\n\n    /// Scan a single shard for drift.\n    async fn scan_shard(&self, topology: &Topology, shard_id: u32) -> Result<bool> {\n        // For each replica group, get the assigned nodes\n        let mut fingerprints = Vec::new();\n\n        for group in topology.groups() {\n            let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n            for node_id in assigned {\n                // Look up node address from topology\n                let topology_guard = self.topology.read().await;\n                let node = topology_guard\n                    .node(&node_id)\n                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"node {} not found\", node_id)))?;\n\n                if !node.is_healthy() {\n                    warn!(\"Node {} is not healthy, skipping fingerprint\", node_id);\n                    continue;\n                }\n\n                let address = node.address.clone();\n                drop(topology_guard);\n\n                match self\n                    .fingerprint_shard(&node_id, shard_id, &self.config.index_uid, &address)\n                    .await\n                {\n                    Ok(fp) => fingerprints.push((node_id, fp)),\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        warn!(\n                            \"Failed to fingerprint shard {} on node {}: {}\",\n                            shard_id, node_id, e\n                        );\n                        // Continue with other nodes\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if fingerprints.is_empty() {\n            // No readable replicas\n            return Ok(false);\n        }\n\n        // Compare fingerprints\n        let reference = &fingerprints[0].1;\n        let mut drift_detected = false;\n\n        for (node_id, fp) in &fingerprints[1..] {\n            if fp.merkle_root != reference.merkle_root {\n                drift_detected = true;\n                debug!(\n                    \"Shard {} drift detected: node {} has different merkle root\",\n                    shard_id, node_id\n                );\n\n                if self.config.auto_repair {\n                    // Perform detailed diff and repair\n                    if let Err(e) = self.repair_shard(shard_id, reference, fp).await {\n                        error!(\n                            \"Failed to repair shard {} on node {}: {}\",\n                            shard_id, node_id, e\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(drift_detected)\n    }\n\n    /// Repair a shard by comparing replicas and applying fixes.\n    async fn repair_shard(\n        &self,\n        shard_id: u32,\n        reference: &ShardFingerprint,\n        target: &ShardFingerprint,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        // In a real implementation, this would:\n        // 1. Compute per-bucket hashes to locate divergent documents\n        // 2. Enumerate divergent primary keys\n        // 3. For each divergent PK:\n        //    a. Check if any replica has _miroir_expires_at <= now (TTL interaction)\n        //    b. If expired: delete from all replicas\n        //    c. Otherwise: pick authoritative version (highest _miroir_updated_at)\n        //    d. Write authoritative version to divergent replicas\n\n        debug!(\n            \"Repairing shard {} on node {}\",\n            shard_id, target.node_id\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get pass history.\n    pub async fn pass_history(&self) -> Vec<ReconcilerPass> {\n        self.pass_history.read().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Get current pass in progress.\n    pub async fn current_pass(&self) -> Option<ReconcilerPass> {\n        self.current_pass.read().await.clone()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Validates that migration is safe given the anti-entropy configuration.\n/// Returns Ok(()) if safe, Err with a descriptive message if not.\n///\n/// Hard refusal policy (plan §15 OP#1): skipping the delta pass while\n/// anti-entropy is disabled provides zero recovery path for documents\n/// written at the cutover boundary. Measured loss rate: ~2% of writes.\n/// This is a hard-coded policy, not a warning.\npub fn validate_migration_safety(\n    ae_config: &AntiEntropyConfig,\n    migration_config: &MigrationConfig,\n) -> std::result::Result<(), MigrationError> {\n    if migration_config.skip_delta_pass && !ae_config.enabled {\n        return Err(MigrationError::UnsafeCutoverNoAntiEntropy);\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Generates a warning if anti-entropy is disabled during active migration.\n/// The caller should log this at warn level.\n///\n/// Even with the delta pass enabled (which provides 0-loss cutover on its own),\n/// disabling anti-entropy means the delta pass is the sole safety mechanism.\n/// Operators should be aware of this reduced redundancy.\npub fn migration_warning_if_ae_disabled(ae_enabled: bool) -> Option<String> {\n    if ae_enabled {\n        return None;\n    }\n    Some(\n        \"Anti-entropy is disabled. Shard migration cutover relies on the delta pass \\\n         as the sole safety mechanism. Any bugs in the delta pass could lead to \\\n         data loss at the cutover boundary. Re-enable anti-entropy for defense-in-depth.\"\n            .to_string(),\n    )\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn    /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n66\t    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n67\t        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n68\t        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n69\t        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n70\t\n71\t        for resp in responses {\n72\t            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n73\t            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n74\t            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n75\t                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n76\t            }\n77\t        }\n78\t\n79\t        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n80\t            total_length / total_docs as f64\n81\t        } else {\n82\t            0.0\n83\t        };\n84\t\n85\t        let n = total_docs as f64;\n86\t        let terms = term_df\n87\t            .into_iter()\n88\t            .map(|(term, df)| {\n89\t                let idf = if df == 0 {\n90\t                    0.0\n91\t                } else {\n92\t                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n93\t                };\n94\t                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n95\t            })\n96\t            .collect();\n97\t\n98\t        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n99\t    }\n100\t}\n101\t\n102\t// SearchRequest {\n340\t    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n341\t    ///\n342\t    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n343\t    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n344\t    /// data to apply pagination.\n345\t    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n346\t        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n347\t\n348\t        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n349\t        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n350\t\n351\t        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n352\t        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n353\t        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n354\t\n355\t        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n356\t        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n357\t\n358\t        // Ensure query is set\n359\t        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n360\t            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n361\t        }\n362\t\n363\t set if provided\n369\t        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n370\t            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n371\t        }\n372\t\n373\t        body\n374\t    }\n375\t}\n376\t\n377\t#[derive(Debug)]\n378\tpub struct ScatterResult {\n379\t    pub shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n380\t    pub failed_shards: HashMap<u32, NodeError>,\n381\t    pub partial: query_seq: u64,\n389\t    rf: usize,\n390\t    shard_count: u32,\n391\t) -> ScatterPlan {\n392\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n393\t\n394\t    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n395\t        Some(g) => g,\n396\t        None => {\n397\t            return ScatterPlan {\n398\t                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n399\t                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n400\t            };\n401\t        }\n402\t    };\n403\t\n404\t    let _covering =
