{\n118\t            enabled: true,\n119\t            backfill_concurrency: default_backfill_concurrency(),\n120\t            backfill_batch_size: default_backfill_batch_size(),\n121\t            throttle_docs_per_sec: 0,\n122\t            verify_before_swap: true,\n123\t            retain_old_index_hours: default_retain_hours(),\n124\t            allowed_windows: Vec::new(),\n125\t        }\n126\t    }\n127\t}\n128\t\n129\t/// Result of the schedule window guard check.\n130\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n131\tpub enum WindowGuardResult {\n132\t    /// Current time is inside an allowed window.\n133\t    Allowed { window: String },\n134\t    /// No windows configured — always allowed.\n135\t    NoRestriction,\n136\t    /// Current time is outside all allowed windows.\n137\t    Denied {\n138\t        utc_now: String,\n139\t        allowed: Vec<String>,\n140\t    },\n141\t}\n142\t\n143\t/// Check whether resharding is allowed at the given UTC minute-of-day.\n144\t///\n145\t/// Returns `Allowed` if `utc_minute` falls inside any configured window,\n146\t/// `NoRestriction` if no windows are configured, or `Denied` otherwise.\n147\tpub fn check_window(utc_minute: u16, config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n148\t    if config.allowed_windows.is_empty() {\n149\t        return WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction;\n150\t    }\n151\t\n152\t    for raw in &config.allowed_windows {\n153\t        let window = match TimeWindow::parse(raw) {\n154\t            Ok(w) => w,\n155\t            Err(_) => continue,\n156\t        };\n157\t        if window.contains(utc_minute) {\n158\t            return WindowGuardResult::Allowed {\n159\t                window: raw.clone(),\n160\t            };\n161\t        }\n162\t    }\n163\t\n164\t    WindowGuardResult::Denied {\n165\t        utc_now: format!(\"{:02}:{:02} UTC\", utc_minute / 60, utc_minute % 60),\n166\t        allowed: config.allowed_windows.clone(),\n167\t    }\n168\t}\n169\t\n170\t/// Check the schedule window against the system clock.\n171\tpub fn check_window_now(config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n172\t    let utc_minute = current_utc_minute();\n173\t    check_window(utc_minute, config)\n174\t}\n175\t\n176\tfn current_utc_minute() -> u16 {\n177\t    let duration = SystemTime::now()\n178\t        .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n179\t        .unwrap_or_default();\n180\t    ((duration.as_secs() / 60) % (24 * 60)) as u16\n181\t}\n182\t\n183\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n184\t// Resharding load simulation\n185\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n186\t\n187\t/// Parameters for a single simulation run.\n188\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n189\tpub struct SimParams {\n190\t    /// Document size in bytes.\n191\t    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n192\t    /// Total corpus size in bytes.\n193\t    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n194\t    /// Incoming write rate in documents per second.\n195\t    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n196\t    /// Number of replica groups.\n197\t    pub replica_groups: u32,\n198\t    /// Replication factor (intra-group copies per shard).\n199\t    pub replication_factor: usize,\n200\t    /// Old shard count (before reshard).\n201\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n202\t    /// New shard count (after reshard).\n203\t    pub new_shards: u32,\n204\t    /// Number of nodes per replica group.\n205\t    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n206\t    /// Backfill throttle in documents per second (0 = unlimited).\n207\t    pub backfill_throttle_dps: u64,\n208\t}\n209\t\n210\t/// Results from a single simulation run.\n211\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n212\tpub struct SimResult {\n213\t    pub label: String,\n214\t    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n215\t    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n216\t    pub total_docs: u64,\n217\t    pub replica_groups: u32,\n218\t    pub replication_factor: usize,\n219\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n220\t    pub new_shards: u32,\n221\t    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n222\t    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n223\t\n224\t    /// Normal steady-state storage across entire cluster (bytes).\n225\t    pub normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n226\t    /// Peak storage during resharding (live + shadow full, bytes).\n227\t    pub peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n228\t    /// Storage amplification factor (peak / normal).\n229\t    pub storage_amplification: f64,\n230\t\n231\t    /// Normal steady-state write rate (actual node writes/sec).\n232\t    pub normal_write_rate: u64,\n233\t    /// Dual-write rate (phase 2 only, no backfill).\n234\t    pub dual_write_rate: u64,\n235\t    /// Peak write rate during backfill + dual-write.\n236\t    pub peak_write_rate: u64,\n237\t    /// Write amplification factor during dual-write only.\n238\t    pub dual_write_amplification: f64,\n239\t    /// Write amplification factor during peak (backfill + dual-write).\n240\t    pub peak_write_amplification: f64,\n241\t\n242\t    /// Backfill duration in seconds (at configured throttle).\n243\t    pub backfill_duration_secs: f64,\n244\t    /// Total bytes written during full reshard operation.\n245\t    pub total_bytes_written: u64,\n246\t\n247\t    /// Per-node peak storage (bytes).\n248\t    pub per_node_peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n249\t    /// Per-node normal storage (bytes).\n250\t    pub per_node_normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n251\t\n252\t    /// Hash distribution stats for old shards.\n253\t    pub old_shard_cv: f64,\n254\t    /// Hash distribution stats for new shards.\n255\t    pub new_shard_cv: f64,\n256\t}\n257\t\n258\t/// Run a resharding load simulation with the given parameters.\n259\t///\n260\t/// This models the six-phase resharding process from plan §13.1 using\n261\t/// the actual routing code to compute shard assignments and estimate\n262\t/// storage/write load. Document keys are synthesized for the corpus size\n263\t/// and routed through the real hash function to measure distribution.\n264\tpub fn simulate(params: &SimParams) -> SimResult {\n265\t    let total_docs = params.corpus_size_bytes / params.doc_size_bytes;\n266\t    let rf = params.replication_factor;\n267\t    let rg = params.replica_groups;\n268\t    let nodes_per_group = params.nodes_per_group;\n269\t\n270\t    // Build a synthetic topology for the simulation.\n271\t    let groups: Vec<Group> = (0..rg)\n272\t        .map(|g| {\n273\t            let mut group = Group::new(g);\n274\t            for n in 0..nodes_per_group {\n275\t                group.add_node(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{}-n{}\", g, n)));\n276\t            }\n277\t            group\n278\t        })\n279\t        .collect();\n280\t\n281\t    // Simulate document distribution across old and new shard counts.\n282\t    // Use the actual router hash to get realistic distribution.\n283\t    let mut old_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.old_shards as usize];\n284\t    let mut new_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.new_shards as usize];\n285\t\n286\t    // Track per-node storage for old and new shard assignments.\n287\t    // Each group stores the full corpus; each node in a group stores its\n288\t    // rendezvous-assigned fraction.\n289\t    let total_nodes = (rg as usize) * nodes_per_group;\n290\t    let mut node_storage_old: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n291\t    let mut node_storage_new: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n292\t\n293\t    for i in 0..total_docs {\n294\t        let key = format!(\"doc-{}\", i);\n295\t        let old_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.old_shards);\n296\t        let new_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.new_shards);\n297\t\n298\t        old_shard_counts[old_shard as usize] += 1;\n299\t        new_shard_counts[new_shard as usize] += 1;\n300\t\n301\t        // For each replica group, assign shard to RF nodes.\n302\t        for (g_idx, group) in groups.iter().enumerate() {\n303\t            let old_targets = assign_shard_in_group(old_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n304\t            let new_targets = assign_shard_in_group(new_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n305\t\n306\t            for node_id in &old_targets {\n307\t                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n308\t                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n309\t                node_storage_old[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n310\t            }\n311\t            for node_id in &new_targets {\n312\t                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n313\t                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n314\t                node_storage_new[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n315\t            }\n316\t        }\n317\t    }\n318\t\n319\t    // Compute distribution coefficients of variation.\n320\t    let old_cv = cv(&old_shard_counts);\n321\t    let new_cv = cv(&new_shard_counts);\n322\t\n323\t    // Normal storage: corpus replicated across RG groups.\n324\t    let normal_storage_bytes = params.corpus_size_bytes * rg as u64;\n325\t    // Peak storage: live + shadow (both fully populated).\n326\t    let peak_storage_bytes = normal_storage_bytes * 2;\n327\t\n328\t    // Per-node storage (max across all nodes).\n329\t    let per_node_normal = node_storage_old.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n330\t    let per_node_peak = per_node_normal + node_storage_new.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n331\t\n332\t    // Write rates.\n333\t    // Normal: each incoming doc → RF × RG actual node writes.\n334\t    let normal_write_rate = params.write_rate_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n335\t    // Dual-write: each incoming doc → 2 × (RF × RG) writes (old + new assignment).\n336\t    let dual_write_rate = normal_write_rate * 2;\n337\t    // Backfill: reads all docs, writes each to new assignment → RF × RG writes/doc.\n338\t    // Plus ongoing dual-writes for new incoming docs.\n339\t    let backfill_write_rate = params.backfill_throttle_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n340\t    let peak_write_rate = dual_write_rate + backfill_write_rate;\n341\t\n342\t    let dual_write_amplification = 2.0;\n343\t    let peak_write_amplification = peak_write_rate as f64 / normal_write_rate as f64;\n344\t\n345\t    // Backfill duration: total docs / throttle rate.\n346\t    let backfill_duration_secs = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n347\t        total_docs as f64 / params.backfill_throttle_dps as f64\n348\t    } else {\n349\t        f64::INFINITY\n350\t    };\n351\t\n352\t    // Total bytes written during reshard:\n353\t    // 1. Dual-write ongoing for the full reshard duration.\n354\t    // 2. Backfill writes of entire corpus.\n355\t    // Approximate: backfill_duration × dual_write_rate + corpus × RF × RG.\n356\t    let total_reshard_write_bytes = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n357\t        let dual_write_bytes =\n358\t            backfill_duration_secs * dual_write_rate as f64 * params.doc_size_bytes as f64;\n359\t        let backfill_bytes = total_docs * rf as u64 * rg as u64 * params.doc_size_bytes;\n360\t        (dual_write_bytes as u64) + backfill_bytes\n361\t    } else {\n362\t        0\n363\t    };\n364\t\n365\t    let storage_amplification = peak_storage_bytes as f64 / normal_storage_bytes as f64;\n366\t\n367\t    SimResult {\n368\t        label: format!(\n369\t            \"{}KB/{}GB/RG{}/RF{}\",\n370\t            params.doc_size_bytes / 1024,\n371\t            params.corpus_size_bytes / (1024 * 1024 * 1024),\n372\t            rg,\n373\t            rf\n374\t        ),\n375\t        doc_size_bytes: params.doc_size_bytes,\n376\t        corpus_size_bytes: params.corpus_size_bytes,\n377\t        total_docs,\n378\t        replica_groups: rg,\n379\t        replication_factor: rf,\n380\t        old_shards: params.old_shards,\n381\t        new_shards: params.new_shards,\n382\t        nodes_per_group,\n383\t        write_rate_dps: params.write_rate_dps,\n384\t\n385\t        normal_storage_bytes,\n386\t        peak_storage_bytes,\n387\t        storage_amplification,\n388\t\n389\t        normal_write_rate,\n390\t        dual_write_rate,\n391\t        peak_write_rate,\n392\t        dual_write_amplification,\n393\t        peak_write_amplification,\n394\t\n395\t        backfill_duration_secs,\n396\t        total_bytes_written: total_reshard_write_bytes,\n397\t\n398\t        per_node_peak_storage_bytes: per_node_peak,\n399\t        per_node_normal_storage_bytes: per_node_normal,\n400\t\n401\t        old_shard_cv: old_cv,\n402\t        new_shard_cv: new_cv,\n403\t    }\n404\t}\n405\t\n406\t/// Coefficient of variation for a distribution.\n407\tfn cv(values: &[u64]) -> f64 {\n408\t    if values.is_empty() {\n409\t        return 0.0;\n410\t    }\n411\t    let n = values.len() as f64;\n412\t    let mean = values.iter().sum::<u64>() as f64 / n;\n413\t    if mean == 0.0 {\n414\t        return 0.0;\n415\t    }\n416\t    let variance = values\n417\t        .iter()\n418\t        .map(|v| (*v as f64 - mean).powi(2))\n419\t        .sum::<f64>()\n420\t        / n;\n421\t    variance.sqrt() / mean\n422\t}\n423\t\n424\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n425\t// Tests\n426\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n427\t\n428\t#[cfg(test)]\n429\tmod tests {\n430\t    use super::*;\n431\t\n432\t    // ---- TimeWindow parsing and containment ----\n433\t\n434\t    #[test]\n435\t    fn time_window_parse_simple() {\n436\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n437\t        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 120);\n438\t        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n439\t    }\n440\t\n441\t    #[test]\n442\t    fn time_window_parse_wrap_midnight() {\n443\t        let w = 01:40\n453\t        assert!(!w.contains(400)); // 06:40\n454\t    }\n455\t\n456\t    #[test]\n457\t    fn time_window_contains_wrap() {\n458\t        let w =    }\n463\t\n464\t    #[test]\n465\t    fn time_window_boundary_start() {\n466\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n467\t        assert!(w.contains(120)); // exactly   #[test]\n537\t    fn simulation_storage_always_2x() {\n538\t        // Regardless of parameters, peak storage should be exactly 2× normal.\n539\t        let params = SimParams {\n540\t cv_empty_and_zero() {\n656\t        assert_eq!(cv(&[]), 0.0);\n657\t        assert_eq!(cv(&[0, 0, 0]), 0.0);\n658\t    }\n659\t}\n660\t\n661\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n662\t// Six-phase resharding execution (plan §13.1)\n663\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n664\t\n665\t/// Resharding phase identifiers (matching plan §13.1 steps).\n666\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n667\t#[repr(u8)]\n668\tpub enum ReshardPhase {\n669\t    /// No active resharding.\n670\t    Idle = 0,\n671\t    /// Phase 1: Shadow index created.\n672\t    ShadowCreated = 1,\n673\t    /// Phase 2: Dual-hash dual-write active.\n674\t    DualWriteActive = 2,\n675\t    /// Phase 3: Backfill in progress.\n676\t    BackfillInProgress = 3,\n677\t    /// Phase 4: Verification in progress.\n678\t    Verifying = 4,\n679\t    /// Phase 5: Alias swap completed.\n680\t    Swapped = 5,\n681\t    /// Phase 6: Cleanup in progress.\n682\t    CleaningUp = 6,\n683\t    /// Resharding completed successfully.\n684\t    Complete = 7,\n685\t    /// Resharding failed.\n686\t    Failed = 8,\n687\t}\n688\t\n689\timpl ReshardPhase {\n690\t    /// Human-readable phase name.\n691\t    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n692\t        match self {\n693\t            Self::Idle => \"Idle\",\n694\t            Self::ShadowCreated => \"Shadow Created\",\n695\t            Self::DualWriteActive => \"Dual-Write Active\",\n696\t            Self::BackfillInProgress => \"Backfill In Progress\",\n697\t            Self::Verifying => \"Verifying\",\n698\t            Self::Swapped => \"Swapped\",\n699\t            Self::CleaningUp => \"Cleaning Up\",\n700\t            Self::Complete => \"Complete\",\n701\t            Self::Failed => \"Failed\",\n702\t        }\n703\t    }\n704\t\n705\t    /// Parse from u8 (for storage).\n706\t    pub fn from_u8(v: u8) -> Option<Self> {\n707\t        match v {\n708\t            0 => Some(Self::Idle),\n709\t            1 => Some(Self::ShadowCreated),\n710\t            2 => Some(Self::DualWriteActive),\n711\t            3 => Some(Self::BackfillInProgress),\n712\t            4 => Some(Self::Verifying),\n713\t            5 => Some(Self::Swapped),\n714\t            6 => Some(Self::CleaningUp),\n715\t            7 => Some(Self::Complete),\n716\t            8 => Some(Self::Failed),\n717\t            _ => None,\n718\t        }\n719\t    }\n720\t}\n721\t\n722\t/// Active resharding operation state.\n723\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n724\tpub struct ReshardOperation {\n725\t    /// Unique operation ID.\n726\t    pub id: String,\n727\t    /// Index UID being resharded.\n728\t    pub index_uid: String,\n729\t    /// Old shard count.\n730\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n731\t    /// New shard count.\n732\t    pub target_shards: u32,\n733\t    /// Current phase.\n734\t    pub phase: ReshardPhase,\n735\t    /// Phase started at (UNIX ms).\n736\t    pub phase_started_at: u64,\n737\t    /// Operation created at (UNIX ms).\n738\t    pub created_at: u64,\n739\t    /// Documents backfilled so far.\n740\t    pub documents_backfilled: u64,\n741\t    /// Total documents to backfill (estimated at start).\n742\t    pub total_documents: u64,\n743\t    /// Last error message (if any).\n744\t    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n745\t    /// Shadow index UID.\n746\t    pub shadow_index: String,\n747\t    /// Verification results (populated after phase 4).\n748\t    pub verification_results: Option<VerificationResults>,\n749\t    /// Cleanup retention deadline (UNIX ms).\n750\t    pub cleanup_deadline: Option<u64>,\n751\t}\n752\t\n753\t/// Results from phase 4 verification.\n754\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n755\tpub struct VerificationResults {\n756\t    /// Live index PK set size.\n757\t    pub live_pk_count: u64,\n758\t    /// Shadow index PK set size.\n759\t    pub shadow_pk_count: u64,\n760\t    /// PKs only in live index.\n761\t    pub live_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n762\t    /// PKs only in shadow index.\n763\t    pub shadow_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n764\t    /// PKs with content hash mismatch.\n765\t    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n766\t    /// Whether verification passed.\n767\t    pub passed: bool,\n768\t}\n769\t\n770\timpl ReshardOperation {\n771\t    /// Create a new resharding operation.\n772\t    pub fn new(index_uid: String, old_shards: u32, target_shards: u32) -> Self {\n773\t        let id = format!(\"reshard-{}-{}\", index_uid, uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n774\t        let shadow_index = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", index_uid, target_shards);\n775\t        let now = millis_now();\n776\t        Self {\n777\t            id,\n778\t            index_uid,\n779\t            old_shards,\n780\t            target_shards,\n781\t            phase: ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n782\t            phase_started_at: now,\n783\t            created_at: now,\n784\t            documents_backfilled: 0,\n785\t            total_documents: 0,\n786\t            last_error: None,\n787\t            shadow_index,\n788\t            verification_results: None,\n789\t            cleanup_deadline: None,\n790\t        }\n791\t    }\n792\t\n793\t    /// Transition to the next phase.\n794\t    pub fn advance_phase(&mut self, new_phase: ReshardPhase) {\n795\t        self.phase = new_phase;\n796\t        self.phase_started_at = millis_now();\n797\t    }\n798\t\n799\t    /// Record an error and mark as failed.\n800\t    pub fn fail(&mut self, error: String) {\n801\t        self.last_error = Some(error);\n802\t        self.phase = ReshardPhase::Failed;\n803\t    }\n804\t\n805\t    /// Update backfill progress.\n806\t    pub fn update_backfill_progress(&mut self, backfilled: u64, total: u64) {\n807\t        self.documents_backfilled = backfilled;\n808\t        self.total_documents = total;\n809\t    }\n810\t\n811\t    /// Calculate backfill progress ratio (0.0 to 1.0).\n812\t    pub fn backfill_progress(&self) -> f64 {\n813\t        if self.total_documents == 0 {\n814\t            return 0.0;\n815\t        }\n816\t        (self.documents_backfilled as f64) / (self.total_documents as f64)\n817\t    }\n818\t\n819\t    /// Check if the operation is in a terminal state.\n820\t    pub fn is_terminal(&self) -> bool {\n821\t        matches!(\n822\t            self.phase,\n823\t            ReshardPhase::Complete | ReshardPhase::Failed\n824\t        )\n825\t    }\n826\t\n827\t    /// Get the shadow index name.\n828\t    pub fn shadow_index_name(&self) -> &str {\n829\t        &self.shadow_index\n830\t    }\n831\t}\n832\t\n833\t/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\n834\tfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n835\t    SystemTime::now()\n836\t        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n837\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n838\t        .as_millis() as u64\n839\t}\n840\t\n841\t/// In-memory registry of active resharding operations.\n842\t///\n843\t/// In production, this is persisted to the task store (SQLite/Redis).\n844\t/// This in-memory version is for single-pod development.\n845\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n846\tpub struct ReshardRegistry {\n847\t    operations: HashMap<String, ReshardOperation>,\n848\t    /// Index UID -> active operation ID (at most one per index).\n849\t    index_ops: HashMap<String, String>,\n850\t}\n851\t\n852\t/// Leader-coordinated reshard coordinator (plan §14.5 Mode B).\n853\t///\n854\t/// Acquires a per-index leader lease (scope: \"reshard:<index>\") and persists\n855\t/// phase state so that a new leader can resume from the last committed phase.\n856\tpub struct ReshardCoordinator<E> {\n857\t    /// Mode B operation leader with phase state persistence.\n858\t    leader: ModeBOpLeader<ReshardExtraState>,\n859\t    /// Phantom for the executor type.\n860\t    _phantom: std::marker::PhantomData<E>,\n861\t}\n862\t\n863\t/// Extra state for reshard operations persisted to mode_b_operations.\n864\t///\n865\t/// # CDC Origin Tag (plan §13.13)\n866\t///\n867\t/// Backfill writes during phase 3 must be tagged with `origin=\"reshard_backfill\"`\n868\t/// so they are suppressed from CDC by default (unless `emit_internal_writes` is true).\n869\t///\n870\t/// When constructing `WriteRequest` for backfill writes, set:\n871\t/// ```ignore\n872\t/// use miroir_core::cdc::ORIGIN_RESHARD_BACKFILL;\n873\t/// WriteRequest { ..., origin: Some(ORIGIN_RESHARD_BACKFILL.to_string()) }\n874\t/// ```\n875\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\n876\tpub struct ReshardExtraState {\n877\t    /// Index UID being resharded.\n878\t    pub index_uid: String,\n879\t    /// Old shard count.\n880\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n881\t    /// New shard count.\n882\t    pub target_shards: u32,\n883\t    /// Shadow index UID.\n884\t    pub shadow_index: String,\n885\t    /// Documents backfilled so far.\n886\t    pub documents_backfilled: u64,\n887\t    /// Total documents to backfill.\n888\t    pub total_documents: u64,\n889\t    /// Last error message.\n890\t    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n891\t}\n892\t\n893\timpl<E> ReshardCoordinator<E> {\n894\t    /// Create a new reshard coordinator.\n895\t    pub fn new(\n896\t        leader_election: Arc<crate::leader_election::LeaderElection>,\n897\t        task_store: Arc<dyn crate::task_store::TaskStore>,\n898\t        index_uid: String,\n899\t        old_shards: u32,\n900\t        target_shards: u32,\n901\t        pod_id: String,\n902\t    ) -> Self {\n903\t        let scope = format!(\"reshard:{}\", index_uid);\n904\t        let shadow_index = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", index_uid, target_shards);\n905\t\n906\t        let extra_state = ReshardExtraState {\n907\t            index_uid,\n908\t            old_shards,\n909\t            target_shards,\n910\t            shadow_index,\n911\t            documents_backfilled: 0,\n912\t            total_documents: 0,\n913\t            last_error: None,\n914\t        };\n915\t\n916\t        let leader = ModeBOpLeader::new(\n917\t            leader_election,\n918\t            task_store,\n919\t            crate::task_store::mode_b_type::RESHARD.to_string(),\n920\t            scope,\n921\t            pod_id,\n922\t            extra_state,\n923\t        );\n924\t\n925\t        Self {\n926\t            leader,\n927\t            _phantom: std::marker::PhantomData,\n928\t        }\n929\t    }\n930\t\n931\t    /// Try to acquire leadership for this reshard operation.\n932\t    ///\n933\t    /// Returns `Ok(true)` if we are now the leader, `Ok(false)` if another\n934\t    /// pod holds the lease, or `Err` if acquisition failed.\n935\t    pub async fn try_acquire_leadership(&mut self) -> Result<bool, String> {\n936\t        self.leader.try_acquire_leadership().await\n937\t            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n938\t    }\n939\t\n940\t    /// Renew the leader lease.\n941\t    ///\n942\t    /// Returns `Ok(true)` if renewed successfully, `Ok(false)` if we lost\n943\t    /// leadership to another pod, or `Err` if renewal failed.\n944\t    pub async fn renew_leadership(&mut self) -> Result<bool, String> {\n945\t        self.leader.renew_leadership().await\n946\t            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n947\t    }\n948\t\n949\t    /// Check if we are currently the leader.\n950\t    pub fn is_leader(&self) -> bool {\n951\t        self.leader.is_leader()\n952\t    }\n953\t\n954\t    /// Get the current phase.\n955\t    pub fn phase(&self) -> &str {\n956\t        self.leader.phase()\n957\t    }\n958\t\n959\t    /// Get the extra state (mutable).\n960\t    pub fn extra_state(&mut self) -> &mut ReshardExtraState {\n961\t        self.leader.extra_state()\n962\t \"Backfill In Progress\" => ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress,\n1019\t                \"Verifying\" => ReshardPhase::Verifying,\n1020\t                \"Swapped\" => ReshardPhase::Swapped,\n1021\t pub fn prune_completed(&mut self, max_age_ms: u64) {\n1110\t        let now = millis_now();\n1111\t        let mut to_remove = Vec::new();\n1112\t        for (id, op) in &self.operations {\n1113\t Online resharding: window guard, simulation model, and six-phase execution.\n//!\n//! Implements the plan §13.1 shadow-index resharding mechanics and §15 OP#3\n//! empirical validation of the 2× transient load caveat.\n//!\n//! Leader coordination (plan §14.5 Mode B):\n//! - Acquires per-index leader lease (scope: \"reshard:<index>\")\n//! - Persists phase state to mode_b_operations table for recovery\n//! - New leaders resume from last committed phase boundary\n\nuse crate::mode_b_coordinator::{ModeBOpLeader, PhaseState};\nuse crate::router::{assign_shard_in_group, shard_for_key};\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse tracing::{info, warn, error};\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Schedule window guard\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// A UTC time window like `\"02:00-06:00\"`.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct TimeWindow {\n    /// Start hour+minute in minutes since midnight UTC.\n    pub start_mins: u16,\n    /// End hour+minute in minutes since midnight UTC.\n    pub end_mins: u16,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for TimeWindow {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"{:02}:{:02}-{:02}:{:02}\",\n            self.start_mins / 60,\n            self.start_mins % 60,\n            self.end_mins / 60,\n            self.end_mins % 60\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl TimeWindow {\n    /// Parse a `\"HH:MM-HH:MM\"` string (UTC).\n    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Result<Self, String> {\n        let (start, end) = s\n            .split_once('-')\n            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM-HH:MM, got {}\", s))?;\n        Ok(TimeWindow {\n            start_mins: Self::parse_hm(start)?,\n            end_mins: Self::parse_hm(end)?,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn parse_hm(hm: &str) -> Result<u16, String> {\n        let (h, m) = hm\n            .split_once(':')\n            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM, got {}\", hm))?;\n        let h: u16 = h.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid hour: {}\", h))?;\n        let m: u16 = m.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid minute: {}\", m))?;\n        if h >= 24 || m >= 60 {\n            return Err(format!(\"time out of range: {}\", hm));\n        }\n        Ok(h * 60 + m)\n    }\n\n    /// Does `utc_minutes` (minutes since midnight UTC) fall inside this window?\n    pub fn contains(&self, utc_minutes: u16) -> bool {\n        if self.start_mins <= self.end_mins {\n            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins && utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n        } else {\n            // Wraps midnight, e.g. 22:00-06:00\n            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins || utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Resharding configuration (plan §13.1 + schedule window guard).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReshardingConfig {\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_backfill_concurrency\")]\n    pub backfill_concurrency: usize,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_backfill_batch_size\")]\n    pub backfill_batch_size: usize,\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub throttle_docs_per_sec: u64,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub verify_before_swap: bool,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_retain_hours\")]\n    pub retain_old_index_hours: u64,\n    /// Allowed schedule windows in `\"HH:MM-HH:MM UTC\"` format.\n    /// Empty means any time is allowed (no restriction).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub allowed_windows: Vec<String>,\n}\n\nfn default_backfill_concurrency() -> usize {\n    4\n}\nfn default_backfill_batch_size() -> usize {\n    1000\n}\nfn default_true() -> bool {\n    true\n}\nfn default_retain_hours() -> u64 {\n    48\n}\n\nimpl Default for ReshardingConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            backfill_concurrency: default_backfill_concurrency(),\n            backfill_batch_size: default_backfill_batch_size(),\n            throttle_docs_per_sec: 0,\n            verify_before_swap: true,\n            retain_old_index_hours: default_retain_hours(),\n            allowed_windows: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of the schedule window guard check.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum WindowGuardResult {\n    /// Current time is inside an allowed window.\n    Allowed { window: String },\n    /// No windows configured — always allowed.\n    NoRestriction,\n    /// Current time is outside all allowed windows.\n    Denied {\n        utc_now: String,\n        allowed: Vec<String>,\n    },\n}\n\n/// Check whether resharding is allowed at the given UTC minute-of-day.\n///\n/// Returns `Allowed` if `utc_minute` falls inside any configured window,\n/// `NoRestriction` if no windows are configured, or `Denied` otherwise.\npub fn check_window(utc_minute: u16, config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n    if config.allowed_windows.is_empty() {\n        return WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction;\n    }\n\n    for raw in &config.allowed_windows {\n        let window = match TimeWindow::parse(raw) {\n            Ok(w) => w,\n            Err(_) => continue,\n        };\n        if window.contains(utc_minute) {\n            return WindowGuardResult::Allowed {\n                window: raw.clone(),\n            };\n        }\n    }\n\n    WindowGuardResult::Denied {\n        utc_now: format!(\"{:02}:{:02} UTC\", utc_minute / 60, utc_minute % 60),\n        allowed: config.allowed_windows.clone(),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Check the schedule window against the system clock.\npub fn check_window_now(config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n    let utc_minute = current_utc_minute();\n    check_window(utc_minute, config)\n}\n\nfn current_utc_minute() -> u16 {\n    let duration = SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    ((duration.as_secs() / 60) % (24 * 60)) as u16\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Resharding load simulation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Parameters for a single simulation run.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SimParams {\n    /// Document size in bytes.\n    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n    /// Total corpus size in bytes.\n    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n    /// Incoming write rate in documents per second.\n    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n    /// Number of replica groups.\n    pub replica_groups: u32,\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group copies per shard).\n    pub replication_factor: usize,\n    /// Old shard count (before reshard).\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    /// New shard count (after reshard).\n    pub new_shards: u32,\n    /// Number of nodes per replica group.\n    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n    /// Backfill throttle in documents per second (0 = unlimited).\n    pub backfill_throttle_dps: u64,\n}\n\n/// Results from a single simulation run.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SimResult {\n    pub label: String,\n    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub replica_groups: u32,\n    pub replication_factor: usize,\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    pub new_shards: u32,\n    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n\n    /// Normal steady-state storage across entire cluster (bytes).\n    pub normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n    /// Peak storage during resharding (live + shadow full, bytes).\n    pub peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n    /// Storage amplification factor (peak / normal).\n    pub storage_amplification: f64,\n\n    /// Normal steady-state write rate (actual node writes/sec).\n    pub normal_write_rate: u64,\n    /// Dual-write rate (phase 2 only, no backfill).\n    pub dual_write_rate: u64,\n    /// Peak write rate during backfill + dual-write.\n    pub peak_write_rate: u64,\n    /// Write amplification factor during dual-write only.\n    pub dual_write_amplification: f64,\n    /// Write amplification factor during peak (backfill + dual-write).\n    pub peak_write_amplification: f64,\n\n    /// Backfill duration in seconds (at configured throttle).\n    pub backfill_duration_secs: f64,\n    /// Total bytes written during full reshard operation.\n    pub total_bytes_written: u64,\n\n    /// Per-node peak storage (bytes).\n    pub per_node_peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n    /// Per-node normal storage (bytes).\n    pub per_node_normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n\n    /// Hash distribution stats for old shards.\n    pub old_shard_cv: f64,\n    /// Hash distribution stats for new shards.\n    pub new_shard_cv: f64,\n}\n\n/// Run a resharding load simulation with the given parameters.\n///\n/// This models the six-phase resharding process from plan §13.1 using\n/// the actual routing code to compute shard assignments and estimate\n/// storage/write load. Document keys are synthesized for the corpus size\n/// and routed through the real hash function to measure distribution.\npub fn simulate(params: &SimParams) -> SimResult {\n    let total_docs = params.corpus_size_bytes / params.doc_size_bytes;\n    let rf = params.replication_factor;\n    let rg = params.replica_groups;\n    let nodes_per_group = params.nodes_per_group;\n\n    // Build a synthetic topology for the simulation.\n    let groups: Vec<Group> = (0..rg)\n        .map(|g| {\n            let mut group = Group::new(g);\n            for n in 0..nodes_per_group {\n                group.add_node(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{}-n{}\", g, n)));\n            }\n            group\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Simulate document distribution across old and new shard counts.\n    // Use the actual router hash to get realistic distribution.\n    let mut old_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.old_shards as usize];\n    let mut new_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.new_shards as usize];\n\n    // Track per-node storage for old and new shard assignments.\n    // Each group stores the full corpus; each node in a group stores its\n    // rendezvous-assigned fraction.\n    let total_nodes = (rg as usize) * nodes_per_group;\n    let mut node_storage_old: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n    let mut node_storage_new: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n\n    for i in 0..total_docs {\n        let key = format!(\"doc-{}\", i);\n        let old_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.old_shards);\n        let new_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.new_shards);\n\n        old_shard_counts[old_shard as usize] += 1;\n        new_shard_counts[new_shard as usize] += 1;\n\n        // For each replica group, assign shard to RF nodes.\n        for (g_idx, group) in groups.iter().enumerate() {\n            let old_targets = assign_shard_in_group(old_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n            let new_targets = assign_shard_in_group(new_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n\n            for node_id in &old_targets {\n                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n                node_storage_old[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n            }\n            for node_id in &new_targets {\n                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n                node_storage_new[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Compute distribution coefficients of variation.\n    let old_cv = cv(&old_shard_counts);\n    let new_cv = cv(&new_shard_counts);\n\n    // Normal storage: corpus replicated across RG groups.\n    let normal_storage_bytes = params.corpus_size_bytes * rg as u64;\n    // Peak storage: live + shadow (both fully populated).\n    let peak_storage_bytes = normal_storage_bytes * 2;\n\n    // Per-node storage (max across all nodes).\n    let per_node_normal = node_storage_old.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n    let per_node_peak = per_node_normal + node_storage_new.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Write rates.\n    // Normal: each incoming doc → RF × RG actual node writes.\n    let normal_write_rate = params.write_rate_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n    // Dual-write: each incoming doc → 2 × (RF × RG) writes (old + new assignment).\n    let dual_write_rate = normal_write_rate * 2;\n    // Backfill: reads all docs, writes each to new assignment → RF × RG writes/doc.\n    // Plus ongoing dual-writes for new incoming docs.\n    let backfill_write_rate = params.backfill_throttle_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n    let peak_write_rate = dual_write_rate + backfill_write_rate;\n\n    let dual_write_amplification = 2.0;\n    let peak_write_amplification = peak_write_rate as f64 / normal_write_rate as f64;\n\n    // Backfill duration: total docs / throttle rate.\n    let backfill_duration_secs = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n        total_docs as f64 / params.backfill_throttle_dps as f64\n    } else {\n        f64::INFINITY\n    };\n\n    // Total bytes written during reshard:\n    // 1. Dual-write ongoing for the full reshard duration.\n    // 2. Backfill writes of entire corpus.\n    // Approximate: backfill_duration × dual_write_rate + corpus × RF × RG.\n    let total_reshard_write_bytes = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n        let dual_write_bytes =\n            backfill_duration_secs * dual_write_rate as f64 * params.doc_size_bytes as f64;\n        let backfill_bytes = total_docs * rf as u64 * rg as u64 * params.doc_size_bytes;\n        (dual_write_bytes as u64) + backfill_bytes\n    } else {\n        0\n    };\n\n    let storage_amplification = peak_storage_bytes as f64 / normal_storage_bytes as f64;\n\n    SimResult {\n        label: format!(\n            \"{}KB/{}GB/RG{}/RF{}\",\n            params.doc_size_bytes / 1024,\n            params.corpus_size_bytes / (1024 * 1024 * 1024),\n            rg,\n            rf\n        ),\n        doc_size_bytes: params.doc_size_bytes,\n        corpus_size_bytes: params.corpus_size_bytes,\n        total_docs,\n        replica_groups: rg,\n        replication_factor: rf,\n        old_shards: params.old_shards,\n        new_shards: params.new_shards,\n        nodes_per_group,\n        write_rate_dps: params.write_rate_dps,\n\n        normal_storage_bytes,\n        peak_storage_bytes,\n        storage_amplification,\n\n        normal_write_rate,\n        dual_write_rate,\n        peak_write_rate,\n        dual_write_amplification,\n        peak_write_amplification,\n\n        backfill_duration_secs,\n        total_bytes_written: total_reshard_write_bytes,\n\n        per_node_peak_storage_bytes: per_node_peak,\n        per_node_normal_storage_bytes: per_node_normal,\n\n        old_shard_cv: old_cv,\n        new_shard_cv: new_cv,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Coefficient of variation for a distribution.\nfn cv(values: &[u64]) -> f64 {\n    if values.is_empty() {\n        return 0.0;\n    }\n    let n = values.len() as f64;\n    let mean = values.iter().sum::<u64>() as f64 / n;\n    if mean == 0.0 {\n        return 0.0;\n    }\n    let variance = values\n        .iter()\n        .map(|v| (*v as f64 - mean).powi(2))\n        .sum::<f64>()\n        / n;\n    variance.sqrt() / mean\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    // ---- TimeWindow parsing and containment ----\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_simple() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 120);\n        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_wrap_midnight() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"22:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 1320);\n        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_contains_normal() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert!(w.contains(180)); // 03:00\n        assert!(!w.contains(100)); // 01:40\n        assert!(!w.contains(400)); // 06:40\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_contains_wrap() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"22:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert!(w.contains(1350)); // 22:30\n        assert!(w.contains(300)); // 05:00\n        assert!(!w.contains(700)); // 11:40\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_boundary_start() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert!(w.contains(120)); // exactly 02:00\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_boundary_end_exclusive() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert!(!w.contains(360)); // exactly 06:00 is excluded\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_invalid_format() {\n        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"not-a-window\").is_err());\n        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"25:00-06:00\").is_err());\n        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"02:60-06:00\").is_err());\n    }\n\n    // ---- Window guard ----\n\n    #[test]\n    fn window_guard_no_restriction() {\n        let config = ReshardingConfig::default();\n        assert_eq!(check_window(0, &config), WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn window_guard_allowed() {\n        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-06:00\".into()],\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let result = check_window(180, &config); // 03:00\n        assert!(matches!(result, WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn window_guard_denied() {\n        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-06:00\".into()],\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let result = check_window(720, &config); // 12:00\n        assert!(matches!(result, WindowGuardResult::Denied { .. }));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn window_guard_multiple_windows() {\n        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-04:00\".into(), \"22:00-23:30\".into()],\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        // In first window.\n        assert!(matches!(\n            check_window(150, &config),\n            WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }\n        ));\n        // In second window.\n        assert!(matches!(\n            check_window(1350, &config),\n            WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }\n        ));\n        // Outside both.\n        assert!(matches!(\n            check_window(720, &config),\n            WindowGuardResult::Denied { .. }\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // ---- Simulation ----\n\n    #[test]\n    fn simulation_storage_always_2x() {\n        // Regardless of parameters, peak storage should be exactly 2× normal.\n        let params = SimParams {\n            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n            corpus_size_bytes: 10 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024, // 10 GB\n            write_rate_dps: 100,\n            replica_groups: 2,\n            replication_factor: 1,\n            old_shards: 64,\n            new_shards: 128,\n            nodes_per_group: 3,\n            backfill_throttle_dps: 10_000,\n        };\n        let result = simulate(&params);\n        assert!(\n            (result.storage_amplification - 2.0).abs() < 0.01,\n            \"expected ~2.0, got {}\",\n            result.storage_amplification\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn simulation_dual_write_is_2x() {\n        let params = SimParams {\n            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n            corpus_size_bytes: 1024 * 1024,\n            write_rate_dps: 100,\n            replica_groups: 2,\n            replication_factor: 1,\n            old_shards: 16,\n            new_shards: 32,\n            nodes_per_group: 3,\n            backfill_throttle_dps: 1000,\n        };\n        let result = simulate(&params);\n        assert!(\n            (result.dual_write_amplification - 2.0).abs() < 0.01,\n            \"expected 2.0, got {}\",\n            result.dual_write_amplification\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn simulation_low_cv_with_many_docs() {\n        // With enough docs, hash distribution CV should be very low (< 5%).\n        let params = SimParams {\n            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n            corpus_size_bytes: 1_000_000 * 1024, // 1M docs × 1KB\n            write_rate_dps: 100,\n            replica_groups: 1,\n            replication_factor: 1,\n            old_shards: 16,\n            new_shards: 64,\n            nodes_per_group: 4,\n            backfill_throttle_dps: 1000,\n        };\n        let result = simulate(&params);\n        assert!(\n            result.old_shard_cv < 0.05,\n            \"old shard CV too high: {}\",\n            result.old_shard_cv\n        );\n        assert!(\n            result.new_shard_cv < 0.05,\n            \"new shard CV too high: {}\",\n            result.new_shard_cv\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_display_roundtrip() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:30-06:45\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(format!(\"{}\", w), \"02:30-06:45\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_missing_dash() {\n        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00\").unwrap_err();\n        assert!(err.contains(\"expected HH:MM-HH:MM\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_invalid_hm() {\n        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"ab:00-06:00\").unwrap_err();\n        assert!(err.contains(\"invalid hour\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_invalid_minute() {\n        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:ab-06:00\").unwrap_err();\n        assert!(err.contains(\"invalid minute\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn check_window_now_no_restriction() {\n        let config = ReshardingConfig::default();\n        assert!(matches!(check_window_now(&config), WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn simulation_throttle_zero_gives_infinity_duration() {\n        let params = SimParams {\n            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n            corpus_size_bytes: 1024 * 1024,\n            write_rate_dps: 100,\n            replica_groups: 1,\n            replication_factor: 1,\n            old_shards: 4,\n            new_shards: 8,\n            nodes_per_group: 2,\n            backfill_throttle_dps: 0,\n        };\n        let result = simulate(&params);\n        assert!(result.backfill_duration_secs.is_infinite());\n        assert_eq!(result.total_bytes_written, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn cv_empty_and_zero() {\n        assert_eq!(cv(&[]), 0.0);\n        assert_eq!(cv(&[0, 0, 0]), 0.0);\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Six-phase resharding execution (plan §13.1)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Resharding phase identifiers (matching plan §13.1 steps).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum ReshardPhase {\n    /// No active resharding.\n    Idle = 0,\n    /// Phase 1: Shadow index created.\n    ShadowCreated = 1,\n    /// Phase 2: Dual-hash dual-write active.\n    DualWriteActive = 2,\n    /// Phase 3: Backfill in progress.\n    BackfillInProgress = 3,\n    /// Phase 4: Verification in progress.\n    Verifying = 4,\n    /// Phase 5: Alias swap completed.\n    Swapped = 5,\n    /// Phase 6: Cleanup in progress.\n    CleaningUp = 6,\n    /// Resharding completed successfully.\n    Complete = 7,\n    /// Resharding failed.\n    Failed = 8,\n}\n\nimpl ReshardPhase {\n    /// Human-readable phase name.\n    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Idle => \"Idle\",\n            Self::ShadowCreated => \"Shadow Created\",\n            Self::DualWriteActive => \"Dual-Write Active\",\n            Self::BackfillInProgress => \"Backfill In Progress\",\n            Self::Verifying => \"Verifying\",\n            Self::Swapped => \"Swapped\",\n            Self::CleaningUp => \"Cleaning Up\",\n            Self::Complete => \"Complete\",\n            Self::Failed => \"Failed\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Parse from u8 (for storage).\n    pub fn from_u8(v: u8) -> Option<Self> {\n        match v {\n            0 => Some(Self::Idle),\n            1 => Some(Self::ShadowCreated),\n            2 => Some(Self::DualWriteActive),\n            3 => Some(Self::BackfillInProgress),\n            4 => Some(Self::Verifying),\n            5 => Some(Self::Swapped),\n            6 => Some(Self::CleaningUp),\n            7 => Some(Self::Complete),\n            8 => Some(Self::Failed),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Active resharding operation state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReshardOperation {\n    /// Unique operation ID.\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Index UID being resharded.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Old shard count.\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    /// New shard count.\n    pub target_shards: u32,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: ReshardPhase,\n    /// Phase started at (UNIX ms).\n    pub phase_started_at: u64,\n    /// Operation created at (UNIX ms).\n    pub created_at: u64,\n    /// Documents backfilled so far.\n    pub documents_backfilled: u64,\n    /// Total documents to backfill (estimated at start).\n    pub total_documents: u64,\n    /// Last error message (if any).\n    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n    /// Shadow index UID.\n    pub shadow_index: String,\n    /// Verification results (populated after phase 4).\n    pub verification_results: Option<VerificationResults>,\n    /// Cleanup retention deadline (UNIX ms).\n    pub cleanup_deadline: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n/// Results from phase 4 verification.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct VerificationResults {\n    /// Live index PK set size.\n    pub live_pk_count: u64,\n    /// Shadow index PK set size.\n    pub shadow_pk_count: u64,\n    /// PKs only in live index.\n    pub live_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// PKs only in shadow index.\n    pub shadow_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// PKs with content hash mismatch.\n    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// Whether verification passed.\n    pub passed: bool,\n}\n\nimpl ReshardOperation {\n    /// Create a new resharding operation.\n    pub fn new(index_uid: String, old_shards: u32, target_shards: u32) -> Self {\n        let id = format!(\"reshard-{}-{}\", index_uid, uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n        let shadow_index = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", index_uid, target_shards);\n        let now = millis_now();\n        Self {\n            id,\n            index_uid,\n            old_shards,\n            target_shards,\n            phase: ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n            phase_started_at: now,\n            created_at: now,\n            documents_backfilled: 0,\n            total_documents: 0,\n            last_error: None,\n            shadow_index,\n            verification_results: None,\n            cleanup_deadline: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Transition to the next phase.\n    pub fn advance_phase(&mut self, new_phase: ReshardPhase) {\n        self.phase = new_phase;\n        self.phase_started_at = millis_now();\n    }\n\n    /// Record an error and mark as failed.\n    pub fn fail(&mut self, error: String) {\n        self.last_error = Some(error);\n        self.phase = ReshardPhase::Failed;\n    }\n\n    /// Update backfill progress.\n    pub fn update_backfill_progress(&mut self, backfilled: u64, total: u64) {\n        self.documents_backfilled = backfilled;\n        self.total_documents = total;\n    }\n\n    /// Calculate backfill progress ratio (0.0 to 1.0).\n    pub fn backfill_progress(&self) -> f64 {\n        if self.total_documents == 0 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n        (self.documents_backfilled as f64) / (self.total_documents as f64)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the operation is in a terminal state.\n    pub fn is_terminal(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(\n            self.phase,\n            ReshardPhase::Complete | ReshardPhase::Failed\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Get the shadow index name.\n    pub fn shadow_index_name(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.shadow_index\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\n/// In-memory registry of active resharding operations.\n///\n/// In production, this is persisted to the task store (SQLite/Redis).\n/// This in-memory version is for single-pod development.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct ReshardRegistry {\n    operations: HashMap<String, ReshardOperation>,\n    /// Index UID -> active operation ID (at most one per index).\n    index_ops: HashMap<String, String>,\n}\n\n/// Leader-coordinated reshard coordinator (plan §14.5 Mode B).\n///\n/// Acquires a per-index leader lease (scope: \"reshard:<index>\") and persists\n/// phase state so that a new leader can resume from the last committed phase.\npub struct ReshardCoordinator<E> {\n    /// Mode B operation leader with phase state persistence.\n    leader: ModeBOpLeader<ReshardExtraState>,\n    /// Phantom for the executor type.\n    _phantom: std::marker::PhantomData<E>,\n}\n\n/// Extra state for reshard operations persisted to mode_b_operations.\n///\n/// # CDC Origin Tag (plan §13.13)\n///\n/// Backfill writes during phase 3 must be tagged with `origin=\"reshard_backfill\"`\n/// so they are suppressed from CDC by default (unless `emit_internal_writes` is true).\n///\n/// When constructing `WriteRequest` for backfill writes, set:\n/// ```ignore\n/// use miroir_core::cdc::ORIGIN_RESHARD_BACKFILL;\n/// WriteRequest { ..., origin: Some(ORIGIN_RESHARD_BACKFILL.to_string()) }\n/// ```\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\npub struct ReshardExtraState {\n    /// Index UID being resharded.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Old shard count.\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    /// New shard count.\n    pub target_shards: u32,\n    /// Shadow index UID.\n    pub shadow_index: String,\n    /// Documents backfilled so far.\n    pub documents_backfilled: u64,\n    /// Total documents to backfill.\n    pub total_documents: u64,\n    /// Last error message.\n    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl<E> ReshardCoordinator<E> {\n    /// Create a new reshard coordinator.\n    pub fn new(\n        leader_election: Arc<crate::leader_election::LeaderElection>,\n        task_store: Arc<dyn crate::task_store::TaskStore>,\n        index_uid: String,\n        old_shards: u32,\n        target_shards: u32,\n        pod_id: String,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let scope = format!(\"reshard:{}\", index_uid);\n        let shadow_index = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", index_uid, target_shards);\n\n        let extra_state = ReshardExtraState {\n            index_uid,\n            old_shards,\n            target_shards,\n            shadow_index,\n            documents_backfilled: 0,\n            total_documents: 0,\n            last_error: None,\n        };\n\n        let leader = ModeBOpLeader::new(\n            leader_election,\n            task_store,\n            crate::task_store::mode_b_type::RESHARD.to_string(),\n            scope,\n            pod_id,\n            extra_state,\n        );\n\n        Self {\n            leader,\n            _phantom: std::marker::PhantomData,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Try to acquire leadership for this reshard operation.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(true)` if we are now the leader, `Ok(false)` if another\n    /// pod holds the lease, or `Err` if acquisition failed.\n    pub async fn try_acquire_leadership(&mut self) -> Result<bool, String> {\n        self.leader.try_acquire_leadership().await\n            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Renew the leader lease.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(true)` if renewed successfully, `Ok(false)` if we lost\n    /// leadership to another pod, or `Err` if renewal failed.\n    pub async fn renew_leadership(&mut self) -> Result<bool, String> {\n        self.leader.renew_leadership().await\n            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we are currently the leader.\n    pub fn is_leader(&self) -> bool {\n        self.leader.is_leader()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current phase.\n    pub fn phase(&self) -> &str {\n        self.leader.phase()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the extra state (mutable).\n    pub fn extra_state(&mut self) -> &mut ReshardExtraState {\n        self.leader.extra_state()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the extra state (immutable).\n    pub fn extra_state_ref(&self) -> &ReshardExtraState {\n        self.leader.extra_state_ref()\n    }\n\n    /// Advance to the next phase and persist state.\n    ///\n    /// Should be called after each phase boundary so that a new leader can\n    /// resume from the last committed phase.\n    pub async fn advance_phase(&mut self, new_phase: ReshardPhase) -> Result<(), String> {\n        let phase_name = new_phase.name().to_string();\n        self.leader.persist_phase(phase_name).await\n            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Update backfill progress and persist.\n    pub async fn update_backfill_progress(\n        &mut self,\n        backfilled: u64,\n        total: u64,\n    ) -> Result<(), String> {\n        self.leader.extra_state().documents_backfilled = backfilled;\n        self.leader.extra_state().total_documents = total;\n        self.leader.persist_phase(self.leader.phase().to_string()).await\n            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Mark the operation as failed and step down from leadership.\n    pub async fn fail(&mut self, error: String) -> Result<(), String> {\n        self.leader.extra_state().last_error = Some(error.clone());\n        self.leader.fail(error).await\n            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Mark the operation as completed and step down from leadership.\n    pub async fn complete(&mut self) -> Result<(), String> {\n        self.leader.complete().await\n            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Recover the operation state from the task store.\n    ///\n    /// Called by a new leader to read the persisted phase state and resume\n    /// from the last committed phase boundary.\n    pub async fn recover(&mut self) -> Result<Option<ReshardPhase>, String> {\n        let existing = self.leader.recover().await\n            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n\n        if let Some(ref op) = existing {\n            // Parse phase string back to ReshardPhase enum\n            let phase = match op.phase.as_str() {\n                \"Idle\" => ReshardPhase::Idle,\n                \"Shadow Created\" => ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n                \"Dual-Write Active\" => ReshardPhase::DualWriteActive,\n                \"Backfill In Progress\" => ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress,\n                \"Verifying\" => ReshardPhase::Verifying,\n                \"Swapped\" => ReshardPhase::Swapped,\n                \"Cleaning Up\" => ReshardPhase::CleaningUp,\n                \"Complete\" => ReshardPhase::Complete,\n                \"Failed\" => ReshardPhase::Failed,\n                _ => {\n                    warn!(\"unknown phase '{}', defaulting to Idle\", op.phase);\n                    ReshardPhase::Idle\n                }\n            };\n\n            info!(\n                index_uid = %self.leader.extra_state_ref().index_uid,\n                phase = %op.phase,\n                \"recovered reshard operation from persisted phase\"\n            );\n\n            return Ok(Some(phase));\n        }\n\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    /// Delete the operation state after completion.\n    pub async fn delete(&self) -> Result<bool, String> {\n        self.leader.delete().await\n            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ReshardRegistry {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new resharding operation.\n    pub fn register(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n        if let Some(existing_id) = self.index_ops.get(&op.index_uid) {\n            return Err(format!(\n                \"Resharding already in progress for index '{}': {}\",\n                op.index_uid, existing_id\n            ));\n        }\n        self.index_ops.insert(op.index_uid.clone(), op.id.clone());\n        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get an operation by ID.\n    pub fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n        self.operations.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get mutable reference for updates.\n    pub fn get_mut(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<&mut ReshardOperation> {\n        self.operations.get_mut(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the active operation for an index (if any).\n    pub fn get_for_index(&self, index_uid: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n        self.index_ops\n            .get(index_uid)\n            .and_then(|id| self.operations.get(id))\n    }\n\n    /// Update an operation.\n    pub fn update(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n        if !self.operations.contains_key(&op.id) {\n            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", op.id));\n        }\n        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Complete an operation and remove from active index tracking.\n    pub fn complete(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n        let op = self.operations.get(id).ok_or_else(|| format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", id))?;\n        if !op.is_terminal() {\n            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' is not in a terminal state\", id));\n        }\n        self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// List all operations.\n    pub fn list(&self) -> Vec<&ReshardOperation> {\n        self.operations.values().collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up completed operations older than the retention period.\n    pub fn prune_completed(&mut self, max_age_ms: u64) {\n        let now = millis_now();\n        let mut to_remove = Vec::new();\n        for (id, op) in &self.operations {\n            if op.is_terminal() && (now.saturating_sub(op.created_at) > max_age_ms) {\n                to_remove.push(id.clone());\n                self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n            }\n        }\n        for id in to_remove {\n            self.operations.remove(&id);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests_reshard_execution {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn phase_display_names() {\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::Idle.name(), \"Idle\");\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress.name(), \"Backfill In Progress\");\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::Failed.name(), \"Failed\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn phase_roundtrip_u8() {\n        for phase in &[\n            ReshardPhase::Idle,\n            ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n            ReshardPhase::DualWriteActive,\n            ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress,\n            ReshardPhase::Verifying,\n            ReshardPhase::Swapped,\n            ReshardPhase::CleaningUp,\n            ReshardPhase::Complete,\n            ReshardPhase::Failed,\n        ] {\n            let v = *phase as u8;\n            assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::from_u8(v), Some(*phase));\n        }\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::from_u8(255), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn operation_creation() {\n        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        assert_eq!(op.index_uid, \"products\");\n        assert_eq!(op.old_shards, 64);\n        assert_eq!(op.target_shards, 128);\n        assert_eq!(op.shadow_index, \"products__reshard_128\");\n        assert_eq!(op.phase, ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated);\n        assert!(!op.is_terminal());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn operation_backfill_progress() {\n        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n        op.total_documents = 1000;\n        op.documents_backfilled = 500;\n        assert!((op.backfill_progress() - 0.5).abs() < f64::EPSILON);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn operation_terminal_states() {\n        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n        assert!(!op.is_terminal());\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n        assert!(op.is_terminal());\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Failed;\n        assert!(op.is_terminal());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_single_op_per_index() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let op1 = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        reg.register(op1).unwrap();\n        let op2 = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 128, 256);\n        assert!(reg.register(op2).is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_get_for_index() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        let id = op.id.clone();\n        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n        let retrieved = reg.get_for_index(\"products\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.id, id);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_complete_releases_index() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        let id = op.id.clone();\n        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n        assert!(reg.get_for_index(\"products\").is_some());\n        let op = reg.get_mut(&id).unwrap();\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n        reg.complete(&id).unwrap();\n        assert!(reg.get_for_index(\"products\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_prune_old_completed() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n        op.created_at = millis_now().saturating_sub(100_000); // 100s ago\n        let id = op.id.clone();\n        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n        reg.prune_completed(50_000); // prune ops older than 50s\n        assert!(reg.get(&id).is_none());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":1227,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1227}}}

/// Start the background sweeper.\n    pub async fn start(&self) {\n        let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n        if *running {\n            return; // Already running\n        }\n        *running = true;\n        drop(running);\n\n        let config = self.config.clone();\n        let state = self.state.clone();\n        let running_flag = self.running.clone();\n\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let mut timer = interval(Duration::from_secs(config.sweep_interval_s));\n            loop {\n                timer.tick().await;\n\n                // Check if still running\n                {\n                    let running = running_flag.read().await;\n                    if !*running {\n                        break;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Run sweep\n                if let Err(e) = Self::run_sweep(&config, &state).await {\n                    tracing::error!(\"TTL sweep failed: {}\", e);\n                }\n            }\n        });\n    }\n\n    /// Stop the background sweeper.\n    pub async fn stop(&self) {\n        let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n        *running = false;\n    }\n\n                \"recovered ILM coordinator from persisted phase\"\n467\t            );\n468\t        }\n469\t\n470\t        Ok(())\n471\t    }\n472\t\n473\t    /// Delete the operation state after  }\n478\t\n479\t    /// Update the last check time and persist.\n480\t    pub async fn update_check_time(&mut self) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n481\t        self.leader.extra_state().last_check_ms =
