ILM (Index Lifecycle Management) — plan §13.17.\n//!\n//! Manages rolling time-series indexes with automatic rollover and retention.\n//! Uses leader-only singleton coordination (plan §14.5) to ensure only one pod\n//! performs rollovers for a given policy.\n//!\n//! # CDC Origin Tag (plan §13.13)\n//!\n//! Rollover writes must be tagged with `origin=\"rollover\"` so they are suppressed\n//! from CDC by default (unless `emit_internal_writes` is true).\n//!\n//! When constructing `WriteRequest` for rollover operations, set:\n//! ```ignore\n//! use miroir_core::cdc::ORIGIN_ROLLOVER;\n//! WriteRequest { ..., origin: Some(ORIGIN_ROLLOVER.to_string()) }\n//! ```\n\nuse crate::leader_election::LeaderElection;\nuse crate::mode_b_coordinator::ModeBOpLeader;\nuse crate::task_store::TaskStore;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{info, error, warn};\n\n/// ILM rollover policy configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RolloverPolicy {\n    /// Policy name.\n    pub name: String,\n    /// Write alias name.\n    pub write_alias: String,\n    /// Read alias name (multi-target).\n    pub read_alias: String,\n    /// Index name pattern with {YYYY-MM-DD} placeholder.\n    pub pattern: String,\n    /// Rollover triggers.\n    pub triggers: RolloverTriggers,\n    /// Retention policy.\n    pub retention: RetentionPolicy,\n    /// Index template reference.\n    pub index_template: IndexTemplate,\n}\n\n/// Triggers that cause a rollover.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RolloverTriggers {\n    /// Maximum documents before rollover.\n    pub max_docs: u64,\n    /// Maximum age before rollover (e.g., \"7d\").\n    pub max_age: String,\n    /// Maximum index size before rollover (GB).\n    pub max_size_gb: u32,\n}\n\n/// Retention policy for old indexes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RetentionPolicy {\n    /// Number of indexes to keep.\n    pub keep_indexes: u32,\n}\n\n/// Index template for rollover.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IndexTemplate {\n    /// Primary key field.\n    pub primary_key: String,\n    /// Named settings profile reference.\n    pub settings_ref: String,\n}\n\n/// ILM manager state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IlmState {\n    /// Registered policies.\n    pub policies: Vec<RolloverPolicy>,\n    /// Active rollover operations.\n    pub active_rollovers: HashMap<String, RolloverOperation>,\n    /// Last check timestamp (UNIX ms).\n    pub last_check_ms: u64,\n}\n\n/// Active rollover operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RolloverOperation {\n    /// Policy name.\n    pub policy_name: String,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: RolloverPhase,\n    /// New index UID.\n    pub new_index: String,\n    /// Old index UID.\n    pub old_index: String,\n    /// Started at (UNIX ms).\n    pub started_at: u64,\n    /// Error message if failed.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Rollover phase.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum RolloverPhase {\n    Creating,\n    FlippingAlias,\n    UpdatingReadAlias,\n    CleaningOld,\n    Complete,\n    Failed,\n}\n\n/// ILM manager — handles index lifecycle for time-series data.\npub struct IlmManager {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: IlmConfig,\n    /// Shared state.\n    state: Arc<RwLock<IlmState>>,\n}\n\n/// ILM manager configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IlmConfig {\n    /// Whether ILM is enabled.\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    /// Check interval (seconds).\n    pub check_interval_s: u64,\n    /// Safety lock: refuse to delete indexes newer than this (days).\n    pub safety_lock_older_than_days: u32,\n    /// Maximum rollovers per check.\n    pub max_rollovers_per_check: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Default for IlmConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            check_interval_s: 3600,\n            safety_lock_older_than_days: 7,\n            max_rollovers_per_check: 10,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl IlmManager {\n    /// Create a new ILM manager.\n    pub fn new(config: IlmConfig) -> Self {\n        let state = Arc::new(RwLock::new(IlmState {\n            policies: Vec::new(),\n            active_rollovers: HashMap::new(),\n            last_check_ms: 0,\n        }));\n\n        if config.enabled {\n            // Spawn background evaluator\n            let state_clone = state.clone();\n            let config_clone = config.clone();\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                Self::background_evaluator(state_clone, config_clone).await;\n            });\n        }\n\n        Self { config, state }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a rollover policy.\n    pub async fn register_policy(&self, policy: RolloverPolicy) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n        state.policies.push(policy);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a policy.\n    pub async fn unregister_policy(&self, name: &str) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n        state.policies.retain(|p| p.name != name);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get all policies.\n    pub async fn policies(&self) -> Vec<RolloverPolicy> {\n        let state = self.state.read().await;\n        state.policies.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Get active rollover for a policy.\n    pub async fn active_rollover(&self, policy_name: &str) -> Option<RolloverOperation> {\n        let state = self.state.read().await;\n        state.active_rollovers.get(policy_name).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Trigger an immediate rollover for a policy.\n    pub async fn trigger_rollover(&self, policy_name: &str) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let state = self.state.read().await;\n        let policy = state.policies.iter()\n            .find(|p| p.name == policy_name)\n            .ok_or_else(|| IlmError::PolicyNotFound(policy_name.to_string()))?;\n\n        // Create rollover operation\n        let now = millis_now();\n        let new_index = Self::format_index_name(&policy.pattern, now);\n        let operation = RolloverOperation {\n            policy_name: policy_name.to_string(),\n            phase: RolloverPhase::Creating,\n            new_index: new_index.clone(),\n            old_index: format!(\"{}-current\", policy.write_alias),\n            started_at: now,\n            error: None,\n        };\n\n        drop(state);\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n        state.active_rollovers.insert(policy_name.to_string(), operation);\n\n        info!(\"ILM: triggered rollover for policy '{}', new index: {}\", policy_name, new_index);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Background evaluator that checks policies and performs rollovers.\n    async fn background_evaluator(\n        state: Arc<RwLock<IlmState>>,\n        config: IlmConfig,\n    ) {\n        info!(\"ILM: background evaluator started\");\n\n        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(config.check_interval_s));\n        loop {\n            interval.tick().await;\n\n            let policies = {\n                let state = state.read().await;\n                state.policies.clone()\n            };\n\n            for policy in policies.iter().take(config.max_rollovers_per_check as usize) {\n                if let Err(e) = Self::evaluate_policy(&state, &policy, &config).await {\n                    error!(\"ILM: error evaluating policy '{}': {}\", policy.name, e);\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Update last check time\n            {\n                let mut state = state.write().await;\n                state.last_check_ms = millis_now();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Evaluate a single policy and perform rollover if needed.\n    async fn evaluate_policy(\n        state: &Arc<RwLock<IlmState>>,\n        policy: &RolloverPolicy,\n        _config: &IlmConfig,\n    ) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        // Check if there's already an active rollover\n        {\n            let state = state.read().await;\n            if state.active_rollovers.contains_key(&policy.name) {\n                return Ok(()); // Skip if rollover in progress\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check triggers (placeholder - would query actual stats in production)\n        let should_rollover = false; // TODO: implement trigger checking\n\n        if should_rollover {\n            // Trigger rollover\n            let now = millis_now();\n            let new_index = Self::format_index_name(&policy.pattern, now);\n            let operation = RolloverOperation {\n                policy_name: policy.name.clone(),\n                phase: RolloverPhase::Creating,\n                new_index,\n                old_index: format!(\"{}-current\", policy.write_alias),\n                started_at: now,\n                error: None,\n            };\n\n            let mut state = state.write().await;\n            state.active_rollovers.insert(policy.name.clone(), operation);\n\n            info!(\"ILM: auto-triggered rollover for policy '{}'\", policy.name);\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Format index name from pattern with date placeholder.\n    fn format_index_name(pattern: &str, timestamp_ms: u64) -> String {\n        use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n\n        // Convert milliseconds to DateTime\n        let timestamp_sec = (timestamp_ms / 1000) as i64;\n        let dt = DateTime::<Utc>::from_timestamp(timestamp_sec, 0)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| DateTime::<Utc>::from_timestamp(0, 0).unwrap());\n\n        let date_str = dt.format(\"%Y-%m-%d\").to_string();\n        pattern.replace(\"{YYYY-MM-DD}\", &date_str)\n    }\n}\n\n/// ILM coordinator with leader-only singleton coordination (plan §14.5).\n///\n/// Acquires a global leader lease (scope: \"ilm\") and persists phase state\n/// so that a new leader can resume from the last committed phase.\npub struct IlmCoordinator {\n    /// Mode B operation leader with phase state persistence.\n    leader: ModeBOpLeader<IlmExtraState>,\n}\n\n/// Extra state for ILM operations persisted to mode_b_operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\npub struct IlmExtraState {\n    /// Active rollover operations (policy_name -> rollover state).\n    pub active_rollovers: HashMap<String, RolloverState>,\n    /// Last check timestamp (UNIX ms).\n    pub last_check_ms: u64,\n    /// Next check time for each policy (policy_name -> UNIX ms).\n    pub next_check_times: HashMap<String, u64>,\n}\n\n/// State of a rollover operation in progress.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RolloverState {\n    /// Policy name.\n    pub policy_name: String,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: String,\n    /// New index UID.\n    pub new_index: String,\n    /// Old index UID.\n    pub old_index: String,\n    /// Started at (UNIX ms).\n    pub started_at: u64,\n    /// Error message if failed.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl IlmCoordinator {\n    /// Create a new ILM coordinator.\n    pub fn new(\n        leader_election: Arc<LeaderElection>,\n        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        pod_id: String,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let extra_state = IlmExtraState::default();\n\n        let leader = ModeBOpLeader::new(\n            leader_election,\n            task_store,\n            crate::task_store::mode_b_type::ILM.to_string(),\n            \"ilm\".to_string(),\n            pod_id,\n            extra_state,\n        );\n\n        Self { leader }\n    }\n\n    /// Try to acquire leadership for ILM operations.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(true)` if we are now the leader, `Ok(false)` if another\n    /// pod holds the lease, or `Err` if acquisition failed.\n    pub async fn try_acquire_leadership(&mut self) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        self.leader.try_acquire_leadership().await\n            .map_err(|e| IlmError::CoordinatorError(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Renew the leader lease.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(true)` if renewed successfully, `Ok(false)` if we lost\n    /// leadership to another pod, or `Err` if renewal failed.\n    pub async fn renew_leadership(&mut self) -> Result<bool, IlmError> {\n        self.leader.renew_leadership().await\n            .map_err(|e| IlmError::CoordinatorError(e.to_string()))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we are currently the leader.\n    pub fn is_leader(&self) -> bool {\n        self.leader.is_leader()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current phase.\n    pub fn phase(&self) -> &str {\n        self.leader.phase()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the extra state (mutable).\n    pub fn extra_state(&mut self) -> &mut IlmExtraState {\n        self.leader.extra_state()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the extra state (immutable).\n    pub fn extra_state_ref(&self) -> &IlmExtraState {\n        self.leader.extra_state_ref()\n    }\n\n    /// Advance to the next phase and persist state.\n    ///\n    /// Should be called after each phase boundary so that a new leader can\n    /// resume from the last committed phase.\n    pub async fn advance_phase(&mut self, new_phase: &str) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        self.leader.persist_phase(new_phase.to_string()).await\n            .map_err(|e| IlmError::CoordinatorError(e.to_string()))\n    }\n\n    /// Start a new rollover operation for a policy.\n    pub async fn start_rollover(\n        &mut self,\n        policy_name: &str,\n        new_index: String,\n        old_index: String,\n    ) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let now = millis_now();\n        let rollover_state = RolloverState {\n            policy_name: policy_name.to_string(),\n            phase: \"creating\".to_string(),\n            new_index,\n            old_index,\n            started_at: now,\n            error: None,\n        };\n\n        self.leader.extra_state().active_rollovers.insert(policy_name.to_string(), rollover_state);\n        self.leader.persist_phase(\"rollover_in_progress\".to_string()).await\n            .map_err(|e| IlmError::CoordinatorError(e.to_string()))?;\n\n        info!(\"ILM: started rollover for policy '{}'\", policy_name);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Complete a rollover operation.\n    pub async fn complete_rollover(&mut self, policy_name: &str) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        self.leader.extra_state().active_rollovers.remove(policy_name);\n        self.leader.persist_phase(\"idle\".to_string()).await\n            .map_err(|e| IlmError::CoordinatorError(e.to_string()))?;\n\n        info!(\"ILM: completed rollover for policy '{}'\", policy_name);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Mark the operation as failed and step down from leadership.\n    pub async fn fail(&mut self, error: String) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        self.leader.fail(error).await\n            .map_err(|e| IlmError::CoordinatorError(e.to_string()))\n    }\n\n    /// Mark the operation as completed and step down from leadership.\n    pub async fn complete(&mut self) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        self.leader.complete().await\n            .map_err(|e| IlmError::CoordinatorError(e.to_string()))\n    }\n\n    /// Recover the operation state from the task store.\n    ///\n    /// Called by a new leader to read the persisted phase state and resume\n    /// from the last committed phase boundary.\n    pub async fn recover(&mut self) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let existing = self.leader.recover().await\n            .map_err(|e| IlmError::CoordinatorError(e.to_string()))?;\n\n        if let Some(ref op) = existing {\n            info!(\n                phase = %op.phase,\n                active_rollovers = self.leader.extra_state_ref().active_rollovers.len(),\n                \"recovered ILM coordinator from persisted phase\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Delete the operation state after completion.\n    pub async fn delete(&self) -> Result<bool, IlmError> {\n        self.leader.delete().await\n            .map_err(|e| IlmError::CoordinatorError(e.to_string()))\n    }\n\n    /// Update the last check time and persist.\n    pub async fn update_check_time(&mut self) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        self.leader.extra_state().last_check_ms = millis_now();\n        self.leader.persist_phase(self.leader.phase().to_string()).await\n            .map_err(|e| IlmError::CoordinatorError(e.to_string()))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the active rollover for a policy.\n    pub fn active_rollover(&self, policy_name: &str) -> Option<RolloverState> {\n        self.leader.extra_state_ref().active_rollovers.get(policy_name).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Get all active rollovers.\n    pub fn active_rollovers(&self) -> HashMap<String, RolloverState> {\n        self.leader.extra_state_ref().active_rollovers.clone()\n    }\n}\n\n/// ILM error types.\n#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]\npub enum IlmError {\n    #[error(\"policy not found: {0}\")]\n    PolicyNotFound(String),\n    #[error(\"rollover failed: {0}\")]\n    RolloverFailed(String),\n    #[error(\"alias error: {0}\")]\n    AliasError(String),\n    #[error(\"safety lock violation: index is {\n    use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n {\n                keep_indexes: 30,\n            },\n            index_template: IndexTemplate {\n                primary_key: \"event_id\".into(),\n                settings_ref: \"logs-settings\".into(),\n            },\n        };\n\n        assert!(manager.register_policy(policy).await.is_ok());\n        let policies = manager.policies().await;\n self.version_cache.read().await;\n92\t            let last_update = self.last_cache_update.read().await;\n93\t            if let (Some(ref cached), Some(last)) = (cache.as_ref(), last_update.as_ref()) {\n94\t                if last.elapsed().as_secs() < self.cache_ttl_secs {\n95\t                    return Ok((**cached).clone());\n96\t                }\n97\t            }\n98\t        }\n99\t\n100\t        // Cache miss or expired - fetch from a node\n101\t        let client = Client::builder()\n102\t            .timeout(Duration::from_secs(2))\n103\t            .build()\n104\t            .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n105\t\n106\t        for address in &self.node_addresses {\n107\t            let url = format!(\"{}/version\", address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n108\t            let response = client\n109\t                .get(&url)\n110\t                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key))\n111\t                .send()\n112\t                .await;\n113\t\n114\t            if let Ok(resp) = response {\n115\t                if resp.status().is_success() {\n116\t                    if let Ok(body) = resp.text().await {\n117\t                        // Update cache\n118\t                        *self.version_cache.write().await = Some(body.clone());\n119\t                        *self.last_cache_update.write().await = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n120\t                        return Ok(body);\n121\t                    }\n122\t                }\n123\t            }\n124\t        }\n125\t\n126\t        Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n127\t    }\n128\t}\n129\t\n130\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n131\t// Local Rate Limiter (for single-pod deployments)\n132\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n133\t\n134\t/// In-memory rate limiter for admin login (local backend only).\n135\t/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n136\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n137\tpub struct LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n138\t    inner: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<LocalAdminRateLimiterInner>>,\n139\t}\n140\t\n141\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n142\tstruct LocalAdminRateLimiterInner {\n143\t    /// Map of IP -> (request_timestamps_ms, failed_count, backoff_until_ms)\n144\t    state: HashMap<String, LocalRateLimitState>,\n145\t}\n146\t\n147\t#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\n148\tstruct LocalRateLimitState {\n149\t    /// Timestamps of recent requests (for sliding window)\n150\t    request_timestamps_ms: Vec<i64>,\n151\t    /// Consecutive failed login attempts\n152\t    failed_count: u32,\n153\t    /// Unix timestamp (ms) when backoff expires\n154\t    backoff_until_ms: Option<i64>,\n155\t}\n156\t\n157\timpl LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n158\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n159\t        Self {\n160\t            inner: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalAdminRateLimiterInner::default())),\n161\t        }\n162\t    }\n163\t\n164\t    /// Check rate limit and exponential backoff.\n165\t    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n166\t    pub fn check(\n167\t        &self,\n168\t        ip: &str,\n169\t        limit: u64,\n170\t        window_ms: u64,\n171\t        failed_threshold: u32,\n172\t        backoff_start_minutes: u64,\n173\t        backoff_max_hours: u64,\n174\t    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n175\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n176\t        let now = now_ms();\n177\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n178\t\n179\t        // Check if we're in backoff mode\n180\t        if let Some(backoff_until) = state.backoff_until_ms {\n181\t            if backoff_until > now {\n182\t                let wait_seconds = ((backoff_until - now) / 1000) as u64;\n183\t                return (false, Some(wait_seconds));\n184\t            }\n185\t            // Backoff expired, clear it\n186\t            state.backoff_until_ms = None;\n187\t        }\n188\t\n189\t        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n190\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n191\t\n192\t        // Check if limit exceeded\n193\t        if state.request_timestamps_ms.len() >= limit as usize {\n194\t            // Enter backoff mode after threshold consecutive failures\n195\t            let failed = state.failed_count + 1;\n196\t            state.failed_count = failed;\n197\t\n198\t            if failed >= failed_threshold {\n199\t                let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((failed - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7)); // Cap at 2^7 = 128x\n200\t                let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n201\t                state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n202\t                return (false, Some(backoff_seconds));\n203\t            }\n204\t\n205\t            return (false, None);\n206\t        }\n207\t\n208\t        // Record this request\n209\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.push(now);\n210\t        (true, None)\n211\t    }\n212\t\n213\t    /// Reset rate limit and backoff state on successful login.\n214\t    pub fn reset(&self, ip: &str) {\n215\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n216\t        inner.state.remove(ip);\n217\t    }\n218\t\n219\t    /// Record a failed login attempt (for backoff calculation).\n220\t    pub fn record_failure(&self, ip: &str, failed_threshold: u32, backoff_start_minutes: u64, backoff_max_hours: u64) -> Option<u64> {\n221\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n222\t        let now = now_ms();\n223\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n224\t\n225\t        state.failed_count += 1;\n226\t\n227\t        if state.failed_count >= failed_threshold {\n228\t            let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((state.failed_count - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7));\n229\t            let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n230\t            state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n231\t            return Some(backoff_seconds);\n232\t        }\n233\t\n234\t        None\n235\t    }\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\timpl Default for LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n239\t    fn default() -> Self {\n240\t        Self::new()\n241\t    }\n242\t}\n243\t\n244\t/// In-memory rate limiter for search UI (local backend only).\n245\t/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n246\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n247\tpub struct LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n248\t    inner: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner>>,\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n252\tstruct LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner {\n253\t    /// Map of IP -> request_timestamps_ms\n254\t    state: HashMap<String, Vec<i64>>,\n255\t}\n256\t\n257\timpl LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n258\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n259\t        Self {\n260\t            inner: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner::default())),\n261\t        }\n262\t    }\n263\t\n264\t    /// Check rate limit for search UI.\n265\t    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n266\t    pub fn check(\n267\t        &self,\n268\t        ip: &str,\n269\t        limit: u64,\n270\t        window_ms: u64,\n271\t    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n272\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n273\t        let now = now_ms();\n274\t        let timestamps = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n275\t\n276\t        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n277\t        timestamps.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n278\t\n279\t        // Check if limit exceeded\n280\t        if timestamps.len() >= limit as usize {\n281\t            return (false, None);\n282\t        }\n283\t\n284\t        // Record this request\n285\t        timestamps.push(now);\n286\t        (true, None)\n287\t    }\n288\t}\n289\t\n290\timpl Default for LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n291\t    fn default() -> Self {\n292\t        Self::new()\n293\t    }\n294\t}\n295\t\n296\t/// Get current time in milliseconds since Unix epoch.\n297\tfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n298\t    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n299\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n300\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n301\t        .as_millis() as i64\n302\t}\n303\t\n304\t/// Shared application state for admin endpoints.\n305\t#[derive(Clone)]\n306\tpub struct AppState {\n307\t    pub config: Arc<MiroirConfig>,\n308\t    pub topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n309\t    pub ready: Arc<RwLock<bool>>,\n310\t    pub metrics: super::super::middleware::Metrics,\n311\t    pub version_state: VersionState,\n312\t    pub task_registry: Arc<TaskRegistryImpl>,\n313\t    pub redis_store: Option<RedisTaskStore>,\n314\t    pub task_store: Option<Arc<dyn TaskStore>>,\n315\t    pub pod_id: String,\n316\t    pub seal_key: SealKey,\n317\t    pub local_rate_limiter: LocalAdminRateLimiter,\n318\t    pub local_search_ui_rate_limiter: LocalSearchUiRateLimiter,\n319\t    pub rebalancer: Option<Arc<Rebalancer>>,\n320\t    pub   /// Alias registry (§13.7).\n334\t    pub alias_registry: Arc<miroir_core::alias::AliasRegistry>,\n335\t    /// Leader election service for Mode B operations (plan §14.5).\n336\t    pub seal_key: SealKey,\n346\t    ) -> Self {\n347\t        Self::with_redis(config, metrics, None, \"unknown\".into(), seal_key)\n348\t    }\n349\t\n350\t    pub fn with_redis(\n351\t        config: Self {\n357\t        // Build initial topology from config\n358\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(\n359\t            config.shards,\n360\t            config.replica_groups,\n361\t            config.replication_factor as usize,\n362\t        );\n363\t\n364\t        for node_config in &config.nodes {\n365\t            let node = Node::new(\n366\t                NodeId::new(node_config.id.clone()),\n367\t                node_config.address.clone(),\n368\t                node_config.replica_group,\n369\t            );\n370\t            // Start nodes in ));\n413\t\n414\t        // Create migration executor for actual HTTP document migration\n415\t        use miroir_core::rebalancer::HttpMigrationExecutor;\n416\t        let migration_executor = ).with_migration_executor(migration_executor));\n426\t\n427\t        // Create rebalancer metrics\n428\t        let rebalancer_metrics =     };\n453\t\n454\t            // Create metrics callback for rebalancer operations\n455\t            let metrics_for_worker = metrics.clone();\n456\t            let rebalancer_metrics_callback:                metrics_for_worker.set_rebalance_in_progress(true);\n460\t                    } else {\n461\t                        metrics_for_worker.set_rebalance_in_progress(false);\n462\t                    }\n463\t                    if let Some(count) = docs_migrated {\n464\t                        metrics_for_worker.inc_rebalance_documents_migrated(count);\n465\t                    }\n466\t                    if let Some(duration) = duration_secs {\n467\t                        metrics_for_worker.observe_rebalance_duration(duration);\n468\t                    }\n469\t                }\n470\t            );\n471\t\n472\t            Some(Arc::new(RebalancerWorker::with_metrics(\n473\t                worker_config,\n474\t                topology_arc.clone(),\n475\t Arc::new(miroir_core::settings::SettingsBroadcast::with_task_store(store.clone()))\n489\t        } else {\n490\t            Arc::new(miroir_core::settings::SettingsBroadcast::new())\n491\t        };\n492\t\n493\t        // Create drift reconciler worker (§13.5) if task store is available\n494\t        let drift_reconciler = if let Some(ref store) = task_store {\n495\t            let       None\n585\t        };\n586\t\n587\t        Self {\n588\t            config: Arc::new(config),\n589\t            topology: topology_arc,\n590\t            ready:       anti_entropy_worker,\n608\t            session_manager,\n609\t            alias_registry,\n610\t            leader_election,\n611\t            mode_c_worker,\n612\t        }\n613\t    }\n614\t\n615\t    /// Mark the service as ready (all nodes reachable).\n616\t    pub async fn mark_ready(&self) {\n617\t        *self.ready.write().await = true;\n618\t        info!(\"Service marked as ready\");\n619\t    }\n620\t\n621\t    /// Check if a covering quorum is reachable.\n622\t    pub async fn check_covering_quorum(&self) -> bool {\n623\t        let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n624\t        let node_map = topo.node_map();\n625\t\n626\t        // For each replica group, check if we have enough healthy nodes\n627\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n628\t            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n629\t            let required = (topo.rf() + 1) / 2; // Simple majority for quorum\n630\t            if healthy.len() < required {\n631\t                return false;\n632\t            }\n633\t        }\n634\t\n635\t        true\n636\t    }\n637\t\n638\t    /// Sync rebalancer metrics to Prometheus (called from health checker).\n639\t    pub async fn sync_rebalancer_metrics_to_prometheus(&self) {\n640\t        if let Some(ref rebalancer) = self.rebalancer {\n641\t            let rebalancer_metrics = rebalancer.metrics.read().await;\n642\t            let in_progress = rebalancer_metrics.rebalance_start_time.is_some();\n643\t            self.metrics.set_rebalance_in_progress(in_progress);\n644\t\n645\t            // Calculate delta for documents migrated counter\n646\t            let current_total = rebalancer_metrics.documents_migrated_total;\n647\t            let previous = self.previous_docs_migrated.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n648\t            if current_total > previous {\n649\t                let delta = current_total - previous;\n650\t                self.metrics.inc_rebalance_documents_migrated(delta);\n651\t                self.previous_docs_migrated.store(current_total, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n652\t            }\n653\t\n654\t            let duration = rebalancer_metrics.current_duration_secs();\n655\t            if duration > 0.0 {\n656\t                self.metrics.observe_rebalance_duration(duration);\n657\t            }\n658\t        }\n659\t    }\n660\t}\n661\t\n662\t/// Response for GET /_miroir/topology (plan §10 JSON shape).\n663\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n664\tpub struct TopologyResponse {\n665\t    pub shards: u32,\n666\t    pub replication_factor: u32,\n667\t    pub nodes: Vec<NodeInfo>,\n668\t    pub degraded_node_count: u32,\n669\t    pub rebalance_in_progress: bool,\n670\t    pub fully_covered: bool,\n671\t}\n672\t\n673\t/// Per-node information in the topology response.\n674\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n675\tpub struct NodeInfo {\n676\t    pub id: String,\n677\t    pub address: String,\n678\t    pub status: String,\n679\t    pub shard_count: u32,\n680\t    pub last_seen_ms: u64,\n681\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n682\t    pub error: Option<String>,\n683\t}\n684\t\n685\t/// Response for GET /_miroir/shards.\n686\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n687\tpub struct ShardsResponse {\n688\t    pub shards: HashMap<String, Vec<String>>, // shard_id -> list of node IDs\n689\t}\n690\t\n691\t/// GET /_miroir/topology — full cluster state per plan §10.\n692\tpub async fn get_topology<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<TopologyResponse>, StatusCode>\n693\twhere\n694\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n695\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n696\t{\n697\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n698\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n699\t\n700\t    // Count degraded nodes\n701\t    let degraded_count = topo.nodes().filter(|n| !n.is_healthy()).count() as u32;\n702\t\n703\t    // Check rebalance status\n704\t    let rebalance_in_progress = if let Some(ref rebalancer) = state.rebalancer {\n705\t        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n706\t        status.in_progress\n707\t    } else {\n708\t        false\n709\t    };\n710\t\n711\t    // Build node info list\n712\t    let nodes: Vec<NodeInfo> = topo\n713\t        .nodes()\n714\t        .map(|n| NodeInfo {\n715\t            id: n.id.as_str().to_string(),\n716\t            address: n.address.clone(),\n717\t            status: format!(\"{:?}\", n.status).to_lowercase(),\n718\t            shard_count: 0, // TODO: compute from routing table\n719\t            last_seen_ms: 0, // TODO: track last health check time\n720\t            error: None,     // TODO: populate from last health check error\n721\t        })\n722\t        .collect();\n723\t\n724\t    // Check if fully covered\n725\t    let fully_covered = degraded_count == 0;\n726\t\n727\t    let response = TopologyResponse {\n728\t        shards: topo.shards,\n729\t        replication_factor: topo.rf() as u32,\n730\t        nodes,\n731\t        degraded_node_count: degraded_count,\n732\t        rebalance_in_progress,\n733\t        fully_covered,\n734\t    };\n735\t\n736\t    Ok(Json(response))\n737\t}\n738\t\n739\t/// GET /_miroir/shards — shard → node mapping table.\n740\tpub async fn get_shards<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<ShardsResponse>, StatusCode>\n741\twhere\n742\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n743\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n744\t{\n745\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n746\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n747\t    let mut shards = HashMap::new();\n748\t\n749\t    // Build shard -> node mapping using rendezvous hash\n750\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n751\t        let mut node_ids = Vec::new();\n752\t\n753\t        // Collect nodes from all replica groups for this shard\n754\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n755\t            let assigned = router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf());\n756\t            for node_id in assigned {\n757\t                node_ids.push(node_id.as_str().to_string());\n758\t            }\n759\t        }\n760\t\n761\t        shards.insert(shard_id.to_string(), node_ids);\n762\t    }\n763\t\n764\t    Ok(Json(ShardsResponse { shards }))\n765\t}\n766\t\n767\t/// GET /_miroir/ready — readiness probe (503 during startup, 200 once ready).\n768\tpub async fn get_ready<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<&'static str, StatusCode>\n769\twhere\n770\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n771\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n772\t{\n773\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n774\t    let ready = *state.ready.read().await;\n775\t\n776\t    if ready {\n777\t        Ok(\"\")\n778\t    } else {\n779\t        // Not yet marked ready - check if covering quorum exists\n780\t        let has_quorum = state.check_covering_quorum().await;\n781\t        if has_quorum {\n782\t            // Auto-mark ready on first successful quorum check\n783\t            state.mark_ready().await;\n784\t            Ok(\"\")\n785\t        } else {\n786\t            Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n787\t        }\n788\t    }\n789\t}\n790\t\n791\t/// GET /_miroir/metrics — admin-key-gated Prometheus metrics.\n792\tpub async fn get_metrics<S>(State(state):        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()\n803\t        }\n804\t    }\n805\t}\n806\t\n807\t/// POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key — manual rotation trigger.\n808\t///\n809\t/// Admin-gated endpoint that initiates a scoped key rotation for the given index.\n810\t/// Set `force: true` in the request body to bypass the timing gate.\n811\tpub async fn rotate_scoped_key_handler<S>(\n812\t    State(state): State<S>,\n813\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n814\t    Json(body): Json<RotateScopedKeyRequest>,\n815\t) -> Result<Json<RotateScopedKeyResponse>, (StatusCode, String)>\n816\twhere\n817\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n818\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n819\t{\n820\t    let app_state = rotation requires Redis task store\".into(),\n826\t        )\n827\t    })?;\n828\t\n829\t    if !app_state.config.search_ui.enabled {\n830\t        return Err((\n831\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n832\t            \"search_ui is not enabled\".into(),\n833\t        ));\n834\t    }\n835\t\n836\t    let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n837\t        config: app_state.config.clone(),\n838\t        redis,\n839\t        pod_id: app_state.pod_id.clone(),\n840\t    };\n841\t\n842\t    info!(\n843\t        index = %index,\n844\t        force = &index, body.force).await {\n850\t        Ok(response) => Ok(Json(response)),\n851\t        Err(e) => {\n852\t            error!(index = %index, error = %e, \"manual scoped key rotation failed\");\n853\t            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e))\n854\t        }\n855\t    }\n856\t}\n857\t\n858\t/// Parse a rate limit string like \"10/minute\" into (limit, window_seconds).\n859\tpub fn parse_rate_limit(s: &str) -> Result<(u64, u64), String> {\n860\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = s.split('/').collect();\n861\t    if parts.len() != 2 {\n862\t        return Err(format!(\"invalid rate limit format: '{}', expected 'N/UNIT'\", s));\n863\t    }\n864\t    let limit: u64 = parts[0].parse()\n865\t        .map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid limit number: '{}'\", parts[0]))?;\n866\t    let window_seconds = match parts[1] {\n867\t        \"second\" | \"s\" => 1,\n868\t        \"minute\" | \"m\" => 60,\n869\t        \"hour\" | \"h\" => 3600,\n870\t        \"day\" | \"d\" => 86400,\n871\t        unit => return Err(format!(\"invalid time unit: '{}', expected second/minute/hour/day\", unit)),\n872\t    };\n873\t    Ok((limit, window_seconds))\n874\t}\n875\t\n876\t/// Generate a random session ID.\n877\tfn generate_session_id() -> String {\n878\t    let mut bytes = [0u8; 24];\n879\t    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n880\t    hex::encode(&bytes)\n881\t}\n882\t\n883\t/// POST /_miroir/admin/login — admin login with rate limiting and exponential backoff.\n884\t///\n885\t/// Request body:\n886\t/// ```json\n887\t/// { \"admin_key\": \"...\" }\n888\t/// ```\n889\t///\n890\t/// On success, sets a `miroir_admin_session` cookie and returns:\n891\t/// ```json\n892\t/// { \"success\": true }\n893\t/// ```\n894\t///\n895\t/// Rate limiting (per source IP):\n896\t/// - 10 requests per minute (configurable via `admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip`)\n897\t/// - After 5 consecutive failed attempts, exponential backoff applies:\n898\t///   - 10m, 20m, 40m, ... up to 24h cap\n899\t///\n900\t/// Successful login resets both the rate limit counter and backoff state.\n901\tpub async fn admin_login<S>(\n902\t    State(state): State<S>,\n903\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n904\t    Json(body): Json<AdminLoginRequest>,\n905\t) -> Response\n906\twhere\n907\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n908\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n909\t{\n910\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n911\t\n912\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n913\t    let source_ip = headers\n914\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n915\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n916\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n917\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n918\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n919\t        .trim()\n920\t        .to_string();\n921\t\n922\t    // Parse rate limit config\n923\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n924\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n925\t        Err(e) => {\n926\t            error!(error = %e, \"invalid admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config\");\n927\t            return (\n928\t                StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n929\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n930\t                    success: false,\n931\t                    message: Some(\"Rate limit configuration error\".into()),\n932\t                }),\n933\t            ).into_response();\n934\t        }\n935\t    };\n936\t\n937\t    // Check rate limit and backoff\n938\t    let backend = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n939\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n940\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n941\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n942\t                Ok((allowed, wait_seconds)) => {\n943\t                    if !allowed {\n944\t                        if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n945\t                            warn!(\n946\t                                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n947\t                                wait_seconds = ws,\n948\t                                \"admin login rate limited (backoff)\"\n949\t                            );\n950\t                            return (\n951\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n952\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n953\t                                    success: false,\n954\t                                    message: Some(format!(\n955\t                                        \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n956\t                                        ws\n957\t                                    )),\n958\t                                }),\n959\t                            ).into_response();\n960\t                        } else {\n961\t                            return (\n962\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n963\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n964\t                                    success: false,\n965\t                                    message: Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into()),\n966\t                                }),\n967\t                            ).into_response();\n968\t                        }\n969\t                    }\n970\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n971\t                }\n972\t                Err(e) => {\n973\t                    error!(error = %e, \"failed to check admin login rate limit\");\n974\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n975\t                }\n976\t            }\n977\t        }\n978\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n979\t        // Local backend rate limiting\n980\t        let (allowed, wait_seconds) = state.local_rate_limiter.check(\n981\t            &source_ip,\n982\t            limit,\n983\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n984\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold,\n985\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes,\n986\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours * 60,\n987\t        );\n988\t        if !allowed {\n989\t            warn!(\n990\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n991\t                wait_seconds = ?wait_seconds,\n992\t                \"admin login rate limited (local backend)\"\n993\t            );\n994\t            return (\n995\t                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n996\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n997\t                    success: false,\n998\t                    message: if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n999\t                        Some(format!(\n1000\t                            \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n1001\t                            ws\n1002\t                        ))\n1003\t                    } else {\n1004\t                        Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into())\n1005\t                    },\n1006\t                }),\n1007\t            ).into_response();\n1008\t        }\n1009\t    }\n1010\t\n1011\t    // Verify admin_key (constant-time comparison to prevent timing side-channels)\n1012\t    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n1013\t    if body.admin_key.as_bytes().ct_eq(state.config.admin.api_key.as_bytes()).into() {\n1014\t        // Successful login - reset rate limit counters\n1015\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n1016\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n1017\t                if let Err(e) = redis.reset_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip) {\n1018\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to reset admin login rate limit\");\n1019\t                }\n1020\t            }\n1021\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n1022\t            state.local_rate_limiter.reset(&source_ip);\n1023\t        }\n1024\t\n1025\t        // Generate session ID and seal it\n1026\t        let session_id = generate_session_id();\n1027\t        let sealed = match seal_session(&session_id, &state.seal_key) {\n1028\t            Ok(sealed) => sealed,\n1029\t            Err(e) => {\n1030\t                error!(error = %e, \"failed to seal admin session\");\n1031\t                return (\n1032\t                    StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n1033\t                    Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1034\t                        success: false,\n1035\t                        message: Some(\"Failed to create session\".into()),\n1036\t                    }),\n1037\t                ).into_response();\n1038\t            }\n1039\t        };\n1040\t\n1041\t        info!(\n1042\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1043\t            session_prefix = &session_id[..8],\n1044\t            \"admin login successful\"\n1045\t        );\n1046\t\n1047\t        // Set cookie and return success\n1048\t        (\n1049\t            StatusCode::OK,\n1050\t            [\n1051\t                (\"Set-Cookie\", format!(\"{}={}; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/; Max-Age={}\",\n1052\t                    COOKIE_NAME, sealed, state.config.admin_ui.session_ttl_s)),\n1053\t            ],\n1054\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1055\t                success: true,\n1056\t                message: None,\n1057\t            }),\n1058\t        ).into_response()\n1059\t    } else {\n1060\t        // Wrong admin_key - record failure for backoff tracking\n1061\t        warn!(\n1062\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1063\t            \"admin login failed: invalid admin_key\"\n1064\t        );\n1065\t\n1066\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n1067\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n1068\t                let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n1069\t                let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n1070\t                let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n1071\t\n1072\t                if let Err(e) = redis.record_failure_admin_login(\n1073\t                    &source_ip,\n1074\t                    failed_threshold,\n1075\t                    backoff_start_minutes,\n1076\t                    backoff_max_hours,\n1077\t                ) {\n1078\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to record admin login failure\");\n1079\t                }\n1080\t            }\n1081\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n1082\t            let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n1083\t            let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n1084\t            let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n1085\t\n1086\t            state.local_rate_limiter.record_failure(\n1087\t                &source_ip,\n1088\t                failed_threshold,\n1089\t                backoff_start_minutes,\n1090\t                backoff_max_hours * 60,\n1091\t            );\n1092\t        }\n1093\t\n1094\t        (\n1095\t            StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED,\n1096\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1097\t                success: false,\n1098\t                message: Some(\"Invalid admin key\".into()),\n1099\t            }),\n1100\t        ).into_response()\n1101\t    }\n1102\t}\n1103\t\n1104\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1105\t// Rebalancer Admin API Endpoints (plan §4)\n1106\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1107\t\n1108\t/// POST /_miroir/nodes — Add a node to a replica group.\n1109\t///\n1110\t/// Request body:\n1111\t/// ```json\n1112\t/// {\n1113\t///   \"id\": \"node-new\",\n1114\t///   \"address\": \"http://node-new:7700\",\n1115\t///   \"replica_group\": 0\n1116\t/// }\n1117\t/// ```\n1118\t///\n1119\t/// Implements plan §2 \"Adding a node to an existing group\":\n1120\t/// 1. Add node to topology in `Joining` state\n1121\t/// 2. Send `NodeAdded` event to rebalancer worker\n1122\t/// 3. Worker computes affected shards and starts migration with leader lease\n1123\tpub async fn add_node<S>(\n1124\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1125\t    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n1126\t) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\n1127\twhere\n1128\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n1129\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n1130\t{\n1131\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n1132\t\n1133\t    let id = body.get(\"id\")\n1134\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n1135\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'id' field\".into()))?\n1136\t        .to_string();\n1137\t\n1138\t    let address = body.get(\"address\")\n1139\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n1140\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'address' field\".into()))?\n1141\t        .to_string();\n1142\t\n1143\t    let replica_group = body.get(\"replica_group\")\n1144\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n1145\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'replica_group' field\".into()))?\n1146\t        as u32;\n1147\t\n1148\t    // Add node to topology\n1149\t    {\n1150\t        let mut topo = app_state.topology.write().await;\n1151\t        // Check if node already exists\n1152\t        let node_id = NodeId::new(id.clone());\n1153\t        if topo.node(&node_id).is_some() {\n1154\t            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,\n1155\t                format!(\"Node {} already exists\", id)));\n1156\t        }\n1157\t        // Check if replica group exists\n1158\t        let group_count = topo.groups().count() as u32;\n1159\t        if replica_group >= group_count {\n1160\t            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,\n1161\t                format!(\"Replica group {} does not exist\", replica_group)));\n1162\t        }\n1163\t        let node = Node::new(node_id, address, replica_group);\n1164\t        topo.add_node(node);\n1165\t    }\n1166\t\n1167\t    // Send event to rebalancer worker (if available)\n1168\t    if let Some(ref worker) = app_state.rebalancer_worker {\n1169\t        let event = TopologyChangeEvent::NodeAdded {\n1170\t            node_id: id.clone(),\n1171\t            replica_group,\n1172\t            index_uid: \"default\".to_string(),\n1173\t        };\n1174\t        if let Err(e) = worker.event_sender().try_send(event) {\n1175\t            error!(error = %e, node_id = %id, \"failed to send NodeAdded event to rebalancer worker\");\n1176\t            return Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n1177\t                format!(\"Failed to queue rebalancing: {}\", e)));\n1178\t        }\n1179\t    }\n1180\t\n1181\t    info!(node_id = %id, replica_group, \"Node addition queued for rebalancing\");\n1182\t    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n1183\t        \"node_id\": id,\n1184\t        \"replica_group\": replica_group,\n1185\t        \"message\": format!(\"Node {} added to replica group {}, rebalancing will start shortly\", id, replica_group),\n1186\t    })))\n1187\t}\n1188\t\n1189\t/// DELETE /_miroir/nodes/{id} — Remove a node from the cluster.\n1190\t///\n1191\t/// Request body (optional):\n1192\t/// ```json\n1193\t/// {\n1194\t///   \"force\": false  // Set to true to bypass draining check\n1195\t/// }\n1196\t/// ```\n1197\t///\n1198\t/// Requires the node to be in `draining` state unless `force=true`.\n1199\t/// Note: This only removes the node from topology. Draining must be completed first.\n1200\tpub async fn remove_node<S>(\n1201\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1202\t    Path(node_id): Path<String>,\n1203\t    Json(body): hash_for_log(value: &str) -> String {\n    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n    value.hash(&mut hasher);\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hasher.finish())\n}\n\n/// Request body for POST /_miroir/admin/login.\n#[derive(Deserialize)]\npub struct AdminLoginRequest {\n    pub admin_key: String,\n}\n\nimpl message: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Version state with cache for fetching Meilisearch version.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct VersionState {\n    pub node_master_key: String,\n    pub node_addresses: Vec<String>,\n    pub version_cache: Arc<RwLock<Option<String>>>,\n    pub last_cache_update: Arc<RwLock<Option<std::time::Instant>>>,\n    pub cache_ttl_secs: u64,\n}\n\nimpl VersionState {\n    pub fn new(node_master_key: String, node_addresses: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            node_master_key,\n            node_addresses,\n            version_cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(None)),\n            last_cache_update: Arc::new(RwLock::new(None)),\n            cache_ttl_secs: 60,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Fetch version from a healthy node, using cache if within TTL.\n    pub async fn get_version(&self) -> Result<String, StatusCode> {\n        // Check cache first\n        {\n            let cache = self.version_cache.read().await;\n            let last_update = self.last_cache_update.read().await;\n            if let (Some(ref cached), Some(last)) = (cache.as_ref(), last_update.as_ref()) {\n                if last.elapsed().as_secs() < self.cache_ttl_secs {\n                    return Ok((**cached).clone());\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Cache miss or expired - fetch from a node\n        let client = Client::builder()\n            .timeout(Duration::from_secs(2))\n            .build()\n            .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n\n        for address in &self.node_addresses {\n            let url = format!(\"{}/version\", address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n            let response = client\n                .get(&url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key))\n                .send()\n                .await;\n\n            if let Ok(resp) = response {\n                if resp.status().is_success() {\n                    if let Ok(body) = resp.text().await {\n                        // Update cache\n                        *self.version_cache.write().await = Some(body.clone());\n                        *self.last_cache_update.write().await = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n                        return Ok(body);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Local Rate Limiter (for single-pod deployments)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// In-memory rate limiter for admin login (local backend only).\n/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n    inner: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<LocalAdminRateLimiterInner>>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct LocalAdminRateLimiterInner {\n    /// Map of IP -> (request_timestamps_ms, failed_count, backoff_until_ms)\n    state: HashMap<String, LocalRateLimitState>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\nstruct LocalRateLimitState {\n    /// Timestamps of recent requests (for sliding window)\n    request_timestamps_ms: Vec<i64>,\n    /// Consecutive failed login attempts\n    failed_count: u32,\n    /// Unix timestamp (ms) when backoff expires\n    backoff_until_ms: Option<i64>,\n}\n\nimpl LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            inner: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalAdminRateLimiterInner::default())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check rate limit and exponential backoff.\n    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n    pub fn check(\n        &self,\n        ip: &str,\n        limit: u64,\n        window_ms: u64,\n        failed_threshold: u32,\n        backoff_start_minutes: u64,\n        backoff_max_hours: u64,\n    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n\n        // Check if we're in backoff mode\n        if let Some(backoff_until) = state.backoff_until_ms {\n            if backoff_until > now {\n                let wait_seconds = ((backoff_until - now) / 1000) as u64;\n                return (false, Some(wait_seconds));\n            }\n            // Backoff expired, clear it\n            state.backoff_until_ms = None;\n        }\n\n        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n        state.request_timestamps_ms.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n\n        // Check if limit exceeded\n        if state.request_timestamps_ms.len() >= limit as usize {\n            // Enter backoff mode after threshold consecutive failures\n            let failed = state.failed_count + 1;\n            state.failed_count = failed;\n\n            if failed >= failed_threshold {\n                let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((failed - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7)); // Cap at 2^7 = 128x\n                let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n                state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n                return (false, Some(backoff_seconds));\n            }\n\n            return (false, None);\n        }\n\n        // Record this request\n        state.request_timestamps_ms.push(now);\n        (true, None)\n    }\n\n    /// Reset rate limit and backoff state on successful login.\n    pub fn reset(&self, ip: &str) {\n        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n        inner.state.remove(ip);\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed login attempt (for backoff calculation).\n    pub fn record_failure(&self, ip: &str, failed_threshold: u32, backoff_start_minutes: u64, backoff_max_hours: u64) -> Option<u64> {\n        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n\n        state.failed_count += 1;\n\n        if state.failed_count >= failed_threshold {\n            let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((state.failed_count - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7));\n            let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n            state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n            return Some(backoff_seconds);\n        }\n\n        None\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// In-memory rate limiter for search UI (local backend only).\n/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n    inner: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner>>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner {\n    /// Map of IP -> request_timestamps_ms\n    state: HashMap<String, Vec<i64>>,\n}\n\nimpl LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            inner: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner::default())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check rate limit for search UI.\n    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n    pub fn check(\n        &self,\n        ip: &str,\n        limit: u64,\n        window_ms: u64,\n    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let timestamps = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n\n        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n        timestamps.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n\n        // Check if limit exceeded\n        if timestamps.len() >= limit as usize {\n            return (false, None);\n        }\n\n        // Record this request\n        timestamps.push(now);\n        (true, None)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current time in milliseconds since Unix epoch.\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\n/// Shared    }\n\n    pub fn with_redis(\n        config: MiroirConfig,\n        metrics: super::super::middleware::Metrics,\n        redis_store: Option<RedisTaskStore>,\n        pod_id: String,\n   ).expect(\"Failed to open SQLite task store\")) as Arc<dyn TaskStore>)\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        };\n\n        // Create rebalancer worker if task store is available\n metrics_for_worker.set_rebalance_in_progress(false);\n                    }\n                    if let Some(count) = docs_migrated {\n
